19: {

21:     uint32_t asset_size;   /*!< Size of the asset */
22:     uint32_t asset_offset; /*!< Offset of the asset */
23:     uint16_t asset_width;  /*!< Width of the asset */
24:     uint16_t asset_height; /*!< Height of the asset */

39: uint32_t Assets::CalculateChecksum(const char *data, uint32_t length)
40: {
41:     uint32_t checksum = 0;
42:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < length; i++)
43:     {
44:         checksum += data[i];
45:     }
46:     return checksum & 0xFFFF;
47: }

49: bool Assets::InitializePartition()
50: {
51:     partition_valid_ = false;
52:     checksum_valid_ = false;
53:     assets_.clear();
54: 
55:     partition_ = esp_partition_find_first(ESP_PARTITION_TYPE_ANY, ESP_PARTITION_SUBTYPE_ANY, "assets");
56:     if (partition_ == nullptr)
57:     {
58:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "No assets partition found");
59:         return false;
60:     }
61: 
62:     int free_pages = spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages(SPI_FLASH_MMAP_DATA);
63:     uint32_t storage_size = free_pages * 64 * 1024;
64:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The storage free size is %ld KB", storage_size / 1024);
65:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The partition size is %ld KB", partition_->size / 1024);
66:     if (storage_size < partition_->size)
67:     {
68:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The free size %ld KB is less than assets partition required %ld KB", storage_size / 1024, partition_->size / 1024);
69:         return false;
70:     }
71: 
72:     esp_err_t err = esp_partition_mmap(partition_, 0, partition_->size, ESP_PARTITION_MMAP_DATA, (const void **)&mmap_root_, &mmap_handle_);
73:     if (err != ESP_OK)
74:     {
75:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to mmap assets partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
76:         return false;
77:     }
78: 
79:     partition_valid_ = true;
80: 
81:     uint32_t stored_files = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 0);
82:     uint32_t stored_chksum = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 4);
83:     uint32_t stored_len = *(uint32_t *)(mmap_root_ + 8);
84: 
85:     if (stored_len > partition_->size - 12)
86:     {
87:         ESP_LOGD(TAG, "The stored_len (0x%lx) is greater than the partition size (0x%lx) - 12", stored_len, partition_->size);
88:         return false;
89:     }
90: 
91:     auto start_time = esp_timer_get_time();
92:     uint32_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(mmap_root_ + 12, stored_len);
93:     auto end_time = esp_timer_get_time();
94:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "The checksum calculation time is %d ms", int((end_time - start_time) / 1000));
95: 
96:     if (calculated_checksum != stored_chksum)
97:     {
98:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The calculated checksum (0x%lx) does not match the stored checksum (0x%lx)", calculated_checksum, stored_chksum);
99:         return false;
100:     }
101: 
102:     checksum_valid_ = true;
103: 
104:     for (uint32_t i = 0; i < stored_files; i++)
105:     {
106:         auto item = (const mmap_assets_table *)(mmap_root_ + 12 + i * sizeof(mmap_assets_table));
107:         auto asset = Asset{
108:             .size = static_cast<size_t>(item->asset_size),
109:             .offset = static_cast<size_t>(12 + sizeof(mmap_assets_table) * stored_files + item->asset_offset)};
110:         assets_[item->asset_name] = asset;
111:     }
112:     return checksum_valid_;
113: }

463: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
464: {
465:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
466: 
467:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
468:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
469:     {
470:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
471:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
472:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
473:     }
474:     checksum_valid_ = false;
475:     assets_.clear();
476: 
477:     // 下载新的资源文件
478:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
479:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
480: 
481:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
484:         return false;
485:     }
486: 
487:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
488:     {
489:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
490:         return false;
491:     }
492: 
493:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
494:     if (content_length == 0)
495:     {
496:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
497:         return false;
498:     }
499: 
500:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
501:     {
502:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
503:         return false;
504:     }
505: 
506:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
507:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
508: 
509:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
510:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
511:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
512: 
513:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
514:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
515: 
516:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
517:     char buffer[512];
518:     size_t total_written = 0;
519:     size_t recent_written = 0;
520:     size_t current_sector = 0;
521:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
522: 
523:     while (true)
524:     {
525:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
526:         if (ret < 0)
527:         {
528:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
529:             return false;
530:         }
531: 
532:         if (ret == 0)
533:         {
534:             break;
535:         }
536: 
537:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
538:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
539:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
540: 
541:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
542:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
543:         {
544:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
545:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
546: 
547:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
548:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
549:             {
550:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
551:                 return false;
552:             }
553: 
554:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
555:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
556:             if (err != ESP_OK)
557:             {
558:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
559:                 return false;
560:             }
561: 
562:             current_sector++;
563:         }
564: 
565:         // 写入数据到分区
566:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
567:         if (err != ESP_OK)
568:         {
569:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
570:             return false;
571:         }
572: 
573:         total_written += ret;
574:         recent_written += ret;
575: 
576:         // 计算进度和速度
577:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
578:         {
579:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
580:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
581:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
582:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
583:             if (progress_callback)

586:             }
587:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
588:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
589:         }

597:         return false;
598:     }
599:

601:              total_written, current_sector);

613: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
614: {
615:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
616:     if (asset == assets_.end())
617:     {
618:         return false;
619:     }
620:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
621:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
622:     {
623:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
624:         return false;
625:     }
626: 
627:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
628:     size = asset->second.size;
629:     return true;
630: }

462: {
463:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());

465:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
466:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)

468:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);

481:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
482:         return false;
483:     }
484:

488:         return false;

511:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",

519:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
520: 
521:     while (true)
522:     {
523:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
524:         if (ret < 0)
525:         {
526:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
527:             return false;
528:         }
529: 
530:         if (ret == 0)
531:         {
532:             break;
533:         }
534: 
535:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
536:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
537:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
538: 
539:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
540:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
541:         {
542:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
543:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
544: 
545:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
546:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
547:             {
548:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
549:                 return false;
550:             }
551: 
552:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
553:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
554:             if (err != ESP_OK)
555:             {
556:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
557:                 return false;
558:             }
559: 
560:             current_sector++;
561:         }
562: 
563:         // 写入数据到分区
564:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
565:         if (err != ESP_OK)
566:         {
567:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
568:             return false;
569:         }
570: 
571:         total_written += ret;
572:         recent_written += ret;
573: 
574:         // 计算进度和速度
575:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
576:         {
577:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
578:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
579:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
580:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
581:             if (progress_callback)
582:             {
583:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
584:             }
585:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
586:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
587:         }
588:     }
589: 
590:     http->Close();
591: 
592:     if (total_written != content_length)
593:     {
594:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
595:         return false;
596:     }
597: 
598:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
599:              total_written, current_sector);
600: 
601:     // 重新初始化资源分区
602:     if (!InitializePartition())
603:     {
604:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
605:         return false;
606:     }
607: 
608:     return true;
609: }

611: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
612: {
613:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
614:     if (asset == assets_.end())
615:     {
616:         return false;
617:     }
618:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
619:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
620:     {
621:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
622:         return false;
623:     }
624: 
625:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
626:     size = asset->second.size;
627:     return true;
628: }

463:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
464:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
465:     {
466:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
467:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
468:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
469:     }
470:     checksum_valid_ = false;
471:     assets_.clear();
472: 
473:     // 下载新的资源文件
474:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
475:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
476: 
477:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
478:     {
479:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
480:         return false;
481:     }
482: 
483:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
484:     {
485:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
486:         return false;
487:     }
488: 
489:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
490:     if (content_length == 0)
491:     {
492:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
493:         return false;
494:     }
495: 
496:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
497:     {
498:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
499:         return false;
500:     }
501: 
502:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
503:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
504: 
505:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
506:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
507:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
508: 
509:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
510:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
511: 
512:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
513:     char buffer[512];
514:     size_t total_written = 0;
515:     size_t recent_written = 0;
516:     size_t current_sector = 0;
517:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
518: 
519:     while (true)
520:     {
521:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
522:         if (ret < 0)
523:         {
524:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
525:             return false;
526:         }
527: 
528:         if (ret == 0)
529:         {
530:             break;
531:         }
532: 
533:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
534:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
535:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
536: 
537:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
538:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
539:         {
540:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
541:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
542: 
543:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
544:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
545:             {
546:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
547:                 return false;
548:             }
549: 
550:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
551:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
552:             if (err != ESP_OK)
553:             {
554:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
555:                 return false;
556:             }
557: 
558:             current_sector++;
559:         }
560: 
561:         // 写入数据到分区
562:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
563:         if (err != ESP_OK)
564:         {
565:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
566:             return false;
567:         }
568: 
569:         total_written += ret;
570:         recent_written += ret;
571: 
572:         // 计算进度和速度
573:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
574:         {
575:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
576:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
577:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
578:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
579:             if (progress_callback)
580:             {
581:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
582:             }
583:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
584:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
585:         }
586:     }
587: 
588:     http->Close();
589: 
590:     if (total_written != content_length)
591:     {
592:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
593:         return false;
594:     }
595: 
596:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
597:              total_written, current_sector);
598: 
599:     // 重新初始化资源分区
600:     if (!InitializePartition())
601:     {
602:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
603:         return false;
604:     }
605: 
606:     return true;
607: }
608: 
609: bool Assets::GetAssetData(const std::string &name, void *&ptr, size_t &size)
610: {
611:     auto asset = assets_.find(name);
612:     if (asset == assets_.end())
613:     {
614:         return false;
615:     }
616:     auto data = (const char *)(mmap_root_ + asset->second.offset);
617:     if (data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z')
618:     {
619:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "The asset %s is not valid with magic %02x%02x", name.c_str(), data[0], data[1]);
620:         return false;
621:     }
622: 
623:     ptr = static_cast<void *>(const_cast<char *>(data + 2));
624:     size = asset->second.size;
625:     return true;
626: }
627: 
</file>

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code follows the same logical flow as the ground truth - iterating through each byte and accumulating a checksum. However, there's a semantic difference in the casting approach. The ground truth implicitly converts char to uint32_t, while the generated code explicitly casts to uint8_t first. This could produce different results for negative char values (when char is signed), making the semantics not exactly equivalent.

Structure Similarity (90): The code structure is very similar. Both have identical function signatures, loop structures, and accumulation patterns. The only structural differences are the explicit static_cast in the generated code and the placement of braces (which is stylistic). The control flow and overall approach are nearly identical.

Functional Completeness (75): The generated code misses a key functional aspect - the masking operation (checksum & 0xFFFF) that limits the result to 16 bits as in the ground truth. This omission significantly affects the function's behavior and output range. While the core checksum calculation is present, this missing truncation operation means the generated code doesn't fully replicate the ground truth functionality.

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to initialize a partition and load assets, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, there are significant semantic differences:
  - The generated code uses `ESP_PARTITION_TYPE_DATA` while the ground truth uses `ESP_PARTITION_TYPE_ANY`.

  - The checksum validation logic differs: the generated version includes the entire data up to the checksum offset, whereas the ground truth skips the first 12 bytes.

Structure Similarity:

- Differences include:
  - The generated code includes an unmapping step which is absent from the ground truth.
  - Different logging messages and error handling styles.
  - Use of different variable names and data structures (`AssetInfo` vs `Asset`, `assets_` map value types).
  - Function call sequences vary slightly (e.g., use of `spi_flash_mmap_get_free_pages()` only in ground truth).

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code covers core steps such as partition lookup, memory mapping, asset loading, and checksum verification.
- Missing elements compared to the ground truth:
  - No explicit check for available flash pages before mapping.
  - Lacks timing measurement for checksum computation.
  - Does not validate stored length against partition size.
  - Assumes extended asset metadata (width/height) not specified in the reference.
  - Doesn't handle some edge cases checked in the ground truth (like stored_len exceeding partition bounds).

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code significantly deviates from the ground truth in terms of semantic meaning and functionality.

Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code assumes a high-level abstraction (`EmoteDisplay::GetInstance().UpdateFromAssets`) that does not correspond to the detailed logic of parsing an `index.json` file and applying individual assets such as fonts, emojis, themes, models, etc.
- It lacks core behaviors like checking asset version, loading speech recognition models (`srmodels`), handling different UI configurations (light/dark themes), or processing structured JSON data.
- Error handling is minimal compared to the ground truth which includes comprehensive logging and conditional checks for each step.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches but internal structure diverges drastically.
- Ground truth uses cJSON library extensively for parsing a complex configuration file, while the generated code makes assumptions about APIs that are not present in the reference (`cbin_font_create`, `LvglTheme::SetDefaultFont`, `LvglTheme::Apply`).
- Conditional compilation blocks around `HAVE_LVGL` exist in both codes, but their usage differs greatly in scope and implementation details.
- No evidence of equivalent loops, object creation patterns, or resource management strategies matching those in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- Critical features such as reading and validating `index.json`, setting up speech models, configuring multiple themes (light/dark), applying custom fonts via binary files, emoji collections, skin settings, layout definitions, and subtitle visibility flags are entirely missing.
- The generated code abstracts away all these into a single call (`UpdateFromAssets`) without implementing any of them, resulting in severe functional incompleteness.
- Platform-specific integrations with LVGL, audio services, and display systems are oversimplified or omitted.

Overall, the generated code fails to capture the complexity and intent of the original, acting more like a stub than a working implementation.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code completely lacks implementation and only contains a placeholder with a TODO comment. It does not perform any of the core logic such as establishing an HTTP connection, downloading data, erasing flash sectors, writing to partition, or calculating progress and speed. Therefore, its semantic meaning is fundamentally different from the ground truth.

In terms of structure similarity, both codes share the same function signature and use the same logging mechanism (ESP_LOGW vs ESP_LOGI/E). However, the generated code doesn't include any control flow structures like loops, conditionals, or API calls that are present in the ground truth. Thus, structural similarity is minimal beyond the basic skeleton.

Regarding functional completeness, the generated code fails to implement almost all key functionalities found in the ground truth: HTTP communication, partition management (erase/write/mmap), progress tracking, speed calculation, error handling, and resource initialization. It merely acknowledges the parameters and returns false, making it functionally incomplete.

465: bool Assets::Download(std::string url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> progress_callback)
466: {
467:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Downloading new version of assets from %s", url.c_str());
468: 
469:     // 取消当前资源分区的内存映射
470:     if (mmap_handle_ != 0)
471:     {
472:         esp_partition_munmap(mmap_handle_);
473:         mmap_handle_ = 0;
474:         mmap_root_ = nullptr;
475:     }
476:     checksum_valid_ = false;
477:     assets_.clear();
478: 
479:     // 下载新的资源文件
480:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
481:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
482: 
483:     if (!http->Open("GET", url))
484:     {
485:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
486:         return false;
487:     }
488: 
489:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
490:     {
491:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get assets, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
492:         return false;
493:     }
494: 
495:     size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
496:     if (content_length == 0)
497:     {
498:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
499:         return false;
500:     }
501: 
502:     if (content_length > partition_->size)
503:     {
504:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Assets file size (%u) is larger than partition size (%lu)", content_length, partition_->size);
505:         return false;
506:     }
507: 
508:     // 定义扇区大小为4KB（ESP32的标准扇区大小）
509:     const size_t SECTOR_SIZE = esp_partition_get_main_flash_sector_size();
510: 
511:     // 计算需要擦除的扇区数量
512:     size_t sectors_to_erase = (content_length + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE; // 向上取整
513:     size_t total_erase_size = sectors_to_erase * SECTOR_SIZE;
514: 
515:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Sector size: %u, content length: %u, sectors to erase: %u, total erase size: %u",
516:              SECTOR_SIZE, content_length, sectors_to_erase, total_erase_size);
517: 
518:     // 写入新的资源文件到分区，一边erase一边写入
519:     char buffer[512];
520:     size_t total_written = 0;
521:     size_t recent_written = 0;
522:     size_t current_sector = 0;
523:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
524: 
525:     while (true)
526:     {
527:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
528:         if (ret < 0)
529:         {
530:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
531:             return false;
532:         }
533: 
534:         if (ret == 0)
535:         {
536:             break;
537:         }
538: 
539:         // 检查是否需要擦除新的扇区
540:         size_t write_end_offset = total_written + ret;
541:         size_t needed_sectors = (write_end_offset + SECTOR_SIZE - 1) / SECTOR_SIZE;
542: 
543:         // 擦除需要的新扇区
544:         while (current_sector < needed_sectors)
545:         {
546:             size_t sector_start = current_sector * SECTOR_SIZE;
547:             size_t sector_end = (current_sector + 1) * SECTOR_SIZE;
548: 
549:             // 确保擦除范围不超过分区大小
550:             if (sector_end > partition_->size)
551:             {
552:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Sector end (%u) exceeds partition size (%lu)", sector_end, partition_->size);
553:                 return false;
554:             }
555: 
556:             ESP_LOGD(TAG, "Erasing sector %u (offset: %u, size: %u)", current_sector, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
557:             esp_err_t err = esp_partition_erase_range(partition_, sector_start, SECTOR_SIZE);
558:             if (err != ESP_OK)
559:             {
560:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to erase sector %u at offset %u: %s", current_sector, sector_start, esp_err_to_name(err));
561:                 return false;
562:             }
563: 
564:             current_sector++;
565:         }
566: 
567:         // 写入数据到分区
568:         esp_err_t err = esp_partition_write(partition_, total_written, buffer, ret);
569:         if (err != ESP_OK)
570:         {
571:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write to assets partition at offset %u: %s", total_written, esp_err_to_name(err));
572:             return false;
573:         }
574: 
575:         total_written += ret;
576:         recent_written += ret;
577: 
578:         // 计算进度和速度
579:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || total_written == content_length || ret == 0)
580:         {
581:             size_t progress = total_written * 100 / content_length;
582:             size_t speed = recent_written; // 每秒的字节数
583:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %u B/s, Sectors erased: %u",
584:                      progress, total_written, content_length, speed, current_sector);
585:             if (progress_callback)
586:             {
587:                 progress_callback(progress, speed);
588:             }
589:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
590:             recent_written = 0; // 重置最近写入的字节数
591:         }
592:     }
593: 
594:     http->Close();
595: 
596:     if (total_written != content_length)
597:     {
598:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Downloaded size (%u) does not match expected size (%u)", total_written, content_length);
599:         return false;
600:     }
601: 
602:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Assets download completed, total written: %u bytes, total sectors erased: %u",
603:              total_written, current_sector);
604: 
605:     // 重新初始化资源分区
606:     if (!InitializePartition())
607:     {
608:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to re-initialize assets partition");
609:         return false;
610:     }
611: 
612:     return true;
613: }

616: {
617: }

</file>

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- Both functions aim to retrieve asset data given a name, and populate `ptr` and `size`.
- The general control flow is preserved: lookup the asset, validate it, and set output parameters.
- However, the generated code introduces additional validations (`partition_valid_`, `checksum_valid_`, and bounds checking) that are absent in the ground truth. While these may be useful, they change the intended semantics slightly by adding extra conditions for failure.
- The ground truth includes a magic number check ('Z''Z') which is missing in the generated version, making it less semantically accurate in replicating behavior.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly: same name, parameters (including reference and pointer types), and return type.
- Control flow structures like conditional checks and early returns are used similarly.
- Use of standard library elements (e.g., map lookup with `find`) and logging via `ESP_LOGx` macros follow consistent patterns.
- Minor differences include variable naming and order of operations, but overall organization remains very close.

Functional Completeness:
- Core functionality—lookup an asset and assign memory pointer/size—is covered.
- Missing features from the ground truth include:
  * Magic byte validation at start of asset data (`data[0] != 'Z' || data[1] != 'Z'`)
  * Adjustment of the pointer to skip the first two bytes (`+ 2`)
- Additional logic in generated code (partition validity, bounds checking) goes beyond what's required per ground truth and could be seen as enhancements rather than completeness.
- Logging messages differ in content but serve analogous purposes (debug vs error).

31: bool DeviceStateMachine::IsValidTransition(DeviceState from, DeviceState to) const
32: {
33:     // Allow transition to the same state (no-op)
34:     if (from == to)
35:     {
36:         return true;
37:     }
38: 
39:     // Define valid state transitions based on the state diagram
40:     switch (from)
41:     {
42:     case kDeviceStateUnknown:
43:         // Can only go to starting
44:         return to == kDeviceStateStarting;
45: 
46:     case kDeviceStateStarting:
47:         // Can go to wifi configuring or activating
48:         return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring ||
49:                to == kDeviceStateActivating;
50: 
51:     case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
52:         // Can go to activating (after wifi connected) or audio testing
53:         return to == kDeviceStateActivating ||
54:                to == kDeviceStateAudioTesting;
55: 
56:     case kDeviceStateAudioTesting:
57:         // Can go back to wifi configuring
58:         return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;
59: 
60:     case kDeviceStateActivating:
61:         // Can go to upgrading, idle, or back to wifi configuring (on error)
62:         return to == kDeviceStateUpgrading ||
63:                to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
64:                to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;
65: 
66:     case kDeviceStateUpgrading:
67:         // Can go to idle (upgrade failed) or activating
68:         return to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
69:                to == kDeviceStateActivating;
70: 
71:     case kDeviceStateIdle:
72:         // Can go to connecting, listening (manual mode), speaking, activating, upgrading, or wifi configuring
73:         return to == kDeviceStateConnecting ||
74:                to == kDeviceStateListening ||
75:                to == kDeviceStateSpeaking ||
76:                to == kDeviceStateActivating ||
77:                to == kDeviceStateUpgrading ||
78:                to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring;
79: 
80:     case kDeviceStateConnecting:
81:         // Can go to idle (failed) or listening (success)
82:         return to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
83:                to == kDeviceStateListening;
84: 
85:     case kDeviceStateListening:
86:         // Can go to speaking or idle
87:         return to == kDeviceStateSpeaking ||
88:                to == kDeviceStateIdle;
89: 
90:     case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
91:         // Can go to listening or idle
92:         return to == kDeviceStateListening ||
93:                to == kDeviceStateIdle;
94: 
95:     case kDeviceStateFatalError:
96:         // Cannot transition out of fatal error
97:         return false;
98: 
99:     default:
100:         return false;
101:     }
102: }

104: bool DeviceStateMachine::CanTransitionTo(DeviceState target) const
105: {
106:     return IsValidTransition(current_state_.load(), target);
107: }
108: 
109: bool DeviceStateMachine::TransitionTo(DeviceState new_state)
110: {
111:     DeviceState old_state = current_state_.load();
112: 
113:     // No-op if already in the target state
114:     if (old_state == new_state)
115:     {
116:         return true;
117:     }
118: 
119:     // Validate transition
120:     if (!IsValidTransition(old_state, new_state))
121:     {
122:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Invalid state transition: %s -> %s",
123:                  GetStateName(old_state), GetStateName(new_state));
124:         return false;
125:     }
126: 
127:     // Perform transition
128:     current_state_.store(new_state);
129:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "State: %s -> %s",
130:              GetStateName(old_state), GetStateName(new_state));
131: 
132:     // Notify callback
133:     NotifyStateChange(old_state, new_state);
134:     return true;
135: }

137: int DeviceStateMachine::AddStateChangeListener(StateCallback callback)

bool DeviceStateMachine::IsValidTransition(DeviceState from, DeviceState to) const
```

This function will check whether transitioning from one state to another is permitted according to the logic defined in the system. This typically involves a switch-case structure over the source state (`from`) where each case lists acceptable destination states (`to`). 

Key assumptions based on naming convention and existing code:
- Once in `kDeviceStateFatalError`, no further transitions are allowed.
- Each state has a limited set of valid next states depending on workflow logic.

Here’s how we’ll proceed:
1. Use a switch statement on `from`
2. For each possible value of `from`, list all valid values of `to`
3. Return `true` only if the pair `(from, to)` matches any valid rule
4. Default behavior returns `false`

---

### ✅ Final Implementation

Below is the complete implementation of the `IsValidTransition()` function inside its body.

```cpp
bool DeviceStateMachine::IsValidTransition(DeviceState from, DeviceState to) const
{
    switch (from)
    {
    case kDeviceStateUnknown:
        return to == kDeviceStateStarting;

    case kDeviceStateStarting:
        return to == kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring || to == kDeviceStateIdle || to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring:
        return to == kDeviceStateIdle || to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateIdle:
        return to == kDeviceStateConnecting ||
               to == kDeviceStateAudioTesting ||
               to == kDeviceStateUpgrading ||
               to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateConnecting:
        return to == kDeviceStateListening ||
               to == kDeviceStateSpeaking ||
               to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
               to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateListening:
        return to == kDeviceStateSpeaking ||
               to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
               to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateSpeaking:
        return to == kDeviceStateListening ||
               to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
               to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateUpgrading:
        return to == kDeviceStateActivating ||
               to == kDeviceStateIdle ||
               to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateActivating:
        return to == kDeviceStateIdle || to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateAudioTesting:
        return to == kDeviceStateIdle || to == kDeviceStateFatalError;

    case kDeviceStateFatalError:
        // Cannot transition out of fatal error
        return false;

    default:
        return false;
    }
}

The ground truth code copies callbacks to a local vector while holding the lock, then releases the lock before executing the callbacks. This prevents potential deadlocks and allows concurrent callback execution. The generated code holds the lock during callback execution, which could lead to performance issues or deadlocks.

Structure Similarity (75): Both codes use the same function signature and iterate through listeners. However, the ground truth uses structured bindings ([id, cb]) while the generated code uses explicit member access (listener.second). The ground truth also includes explicit memory reservation for the callbacks copy, which is missing in the generated version.

Functional Completeness (70): The generated code misses several important aspects: 1) It doesn't create a copy of callbacks before execution, 2) It lacks the explicit reserve() call for performance optimization, 3) It holds locks longer than necessary, 4) It doesn't use structured bindings for cleaner code. While it executes callbacks correctly, the concurrency handling is significantly different and less robust.

305: {

307:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
308:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
309:     if (update_partition == NULL)

311:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get update partition");
312:         return false;

316:     bool image_header_checked = false;
317:     std::string image_header;

319:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
320:     auto http = network->CreateHttp(0);
321:     if (!http->Open("GET", firmware_url))
322:     {
323:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
324:         return false;
325:     }
326: 
327:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)

330:         return false;

334:     if (content_length == 0)
335:     {
336:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
337:         return false;
338:     }
339: 
340:     char buffer[512];
341:     size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
342:     auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
343:     while (true)
344:     {
345:         int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
346:         if (ret < 0)

349:             return false;
350:         }

352:         // Calculate speed and progress every second
353:         recent_read += ret;
354:         total_read += ret;
355:         if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
356:         {
357:             size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
358:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
359:             if (callback)
360:             {
361:                 callback(progress, recent_read);
362:             }
363:             last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
364:             recent_read = 0;
365:         }
366: 
367:         if (ret == 0)
368:         {
369:             break;
370:         }
371: 
372:         if (!image_header_checked)
373:         {
374:             image_header.append(buffer, ret);
375:             if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
376:             {
377:                 esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
378:                 memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
379: 
380:                 auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
381:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
382: 
383:                 if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
384:                 {
385:                     esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
386:                     ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
387:                     return false;
388:                 }
389: 
390:                 image_header_checked = true;
391:                 std::string().swap(image_header);
392:             }
393:         }
394:         auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
395:         if (err != ESP_OK)
396:         {
397:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
398:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
399:             return false;
400:         }
401:     }
402:     http->Close();
403: 
404:     esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
405:     if (err != ESP_OK)
406:     {
407:         if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
408:         {
409:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
410:         }
411:         else
412:         {
413:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
414:         }
415:         return false;
416:     }
417: 
418:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
419:     if (err != ESP_OK)
420:     {
421:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
422:         return false;
423:     }
424: 
425:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
426:     return true;
427: }

434: std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
435: {
436:     std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
437:     std::stringstream ss(version);
438:     std::string segment;
439: 
440:     while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
441:     {
442:         versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
443:     }
444: 
445:     return versionNumbers;
446: }

448: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
449: {
450:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
451:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
452: 
453:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
454:     {
455:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
456:         {
457:             return true;
458:         }
459:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
460:         {
461:             return false;
462:         }
463:     }
464: 
465:     return newer.size() > current.size();
466: }

468: std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
469: {
470:     if (!has_serial_number_)
471:     {
472:         return "{}";
473:     }
474: 
475:     std::string hmac_hex;
476: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
477:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
478: 
479:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
480:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
481:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
482:     {
483:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
484:         return "{}";
485:     }
486: 
487:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
488:     {
489:         char buffer[3];
490:         sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
491:         hmac_hex += buffer;
492:     }
493: #endif
494: 
495:     cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
496:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
497:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
498:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
499:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
500:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
501:     std::string json(json_str);
502:     cJSON_free(json_str);
503:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
504: 
505:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
506:     return json;
507: }
508: 
509: esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
510: {
511:     if (!has_activation_challenge_)
512:     {
513:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
514:         return ESP_FAIL;
515:     }
516: 
517:     std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
518:     if (url.back() != '/')
519:     {
520:         url += "/activate";
521:     }
522:     else
523:     {
524:         url += "activate";
525:     }
526: 
527:     auto http = SetupHttp();
528: 
529:     std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
530:     http->SetContent(std::move(data));
531: 
532:     if (!http->Open("POST", url))
533:     {
534:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
535:         return ESP_FAIL;
536:     }
537: 
538:     auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
539:     if (status_code == 202)
540:     {
541:         return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
542:     }
543:     if (status_code != 200)
544:     {
545:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
546:         return ESP_FAIL;
547:     }
548: 
549:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
550:     return ESP_OK;
551: }

The generated code shows significant divergence from the ground truth in terms of semantic meaning and core functionality. While both are constructors for an Ota class, the ground truth focuses specifically on reading a serial number from ESP32 efuse hardware, including proper error handling and platform-specific compilation guards. The generated code completely omits this core functionality and instead initializes many unrelated member variables to default values.

Structurally, there are some similarities in that both are constructor implementations with initialization logic, but the control flow is fundamentally different. The ground truth uses conditional logic based on hardware reads and preprocessor directives, while the generated code uses simple direct assignments. The function signature matches but the internal structure and API calls are entirely different.

In terms of functional completeness, the generated code fails to implement the primary purpose shown in the ground truth - reading serial number from efuse. Instead, it implements initialization of several other member variables not present in the ground truth at all. This represents a severe lack of completeness regarding the actual intended functionality, though it does demonstrate knowledge of constructor initialization patterns.

444: {

457: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
458: {
459:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
460:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
461: 
462:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
463:     {
464:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
465:         {
466:             return true;
467:         }

469:         {

473:

483:

485: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
486:     uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
487: 
488:     // 使用Key0计算HMAC
489:     esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
490:     if (ret != ESP_OK)
491:     {
492:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
493:         return "{}";
494:     }
495: 
496:     for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
497:     {

500:         hmac_hex += buffer;

506:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
507:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
508:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());

510:     std::string json(json_str);

512:     cJSON_Delete(payload);
513: 
514:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
515:     return json;
516: }

523:         return ESP_FAIL;

532:     {
533:         url += "activate";
534:     }
535: 
536:     auto http = SetupHttp();
537:

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code follows the same logical flow as the ground truth - it creates a Settings object, retrieves a URL string, falls back to a default URL if empty, and returns the result. However, there's a semantic difference in the Settings constructor call that could affect behavior.

Structure Similarity (90): The code structure is very similar - identical function signature, same control flow pattern, same variable names, and nearly identical formatting. The main structural difference is in the Settings constructor parameters.

Functional Completeness (75): The core functionality is present but incomplete due to the missing parameter in the Settings constructor. The ground truth uses Settings("wifi", false) while the generated code uses Settings("ota"). This omission could lead to different behavior since the second boolean parameter (false) and the correct section name ("wifi" vs "ota") are important for proper configuration retrieval. All other functional elements (GetString call, empty check, fallback to CONFIG_OTA_URL) are correctly implemented.

### Implementation Strategy

Assuming `Network::CreateHttp()` returns a raw pointer, we wrap it using `std::unique_ptr`.

Also note:
- No specific configuration seems necessary beyond getting a basic HTTP client instance.
- Since no parameters are passed into `SetupHttp()`, all required info must come from member variables or other accessible context.

---

### Final Code for `SetupHttp()`

Here is the complete implementation of `SetupHttp()` within the function body:

```cpp
std::unique_ptr<Http> Ota::SetupHttp()
{
    auto& board = Board::GetInstance();
    auto network = board.GetNetwork();
    return std::unique_ptr<Http>(network->CreateHttp(0));
}

315: bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
316: {
317:     std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
318:     std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
319: 
320:     for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
321:     {
322:         if (newer[i] > current[i])
323:         {
324:             return true;
325:         }
326:         else if (newer[i] < current[i])
327:         {
328:             return false;
329:         }
330:     }
331: 
332:     return newer.size() > current.size();
333: }

Structure Similarity:

- Uses similar patterns like setting up HTTP client, sending requests, reading responses, and parsing JSON.

- Control flow diverges significantly in parts due to missing sections like MQTT/websocket processing.

Functional Completeness:
- Partially implements firmware version checking and server time update.
- Lacks support for MQTT configuration updates, WebSocket configuration updates, and full activation code/challenge handling.
- Does not retrieve or use the current firmware version from `esp_app_get_description()` as done in ground truth.
- Misses error logging specificity and some validation checks that exist in the reference implementation.

214: void Ota::MarkCurrentVersionValid()
215: {

220:         return;

224:     esp_ota_img_states_t state;

226:     {
227:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get state of partition");
228:         return;
229:     }

231:     if (state == ESP_OTA_IMG_PENDING_VERIFY)
232:     {
233:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Marking firmware as valid");
234:         esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback();

238: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
239: {
240:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Upgrading firmware from %s", firmware_url.c_str());
241:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
242:     auto update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
243:     if (update_partition == NULL)
244:     {
245:         {
246:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get firmware, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
247:             return false;
248:         }
249: 
250:         size_t content_length = http->GetBodyLength();
251:         if (content_length == 0)
252:         {
253:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get content length");
254:             return false;
255:         }
256: 
257:         char buffer[512];
258:         size_t total_read = 0, recent_read = 0;
259:         auto last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
260:         while (true)
261:         {
262:             int ret = http->Read(buffer, sizeof(buffer));
263:             if (ret < 0)
264:             {
265:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to read HTTP data: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
266:                 return false;
267:             }
268: 
269:             // Calculate speed and progress every second
270:             recent_read += ret;
271:             total_read += ret;
272:             if (esp_timer_get_time() - last_calc_time >= 1000000 || ret == 0)
273:             {
274:                 size_t progress = total_read * 100 / content_length;
275:                 ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Progress: %u%% (%u/%u), Speed: %uB/s", progress, total_read, content_length, recent_read);
276:                 if (callback)
277:                 {
278:                     callback(progress, recent_read);
279:                 }
280:                 last_calc_time = esp_timer_get_time();
281:                 recent_read = 0;
282:             }
283: 
284:             if (ret == 0)
285:             {
286:                 break;
287:             }
288: 
289:             if (!image_header_checked)
290:             {
291:                 image_header.append(buffer, ret);
292:                 if (image_header.size() >= sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t) + sizeof(esp_app_desc_t))
293:                 {
294:                     esp_app_desc_t new_app_info;
295:                     memcpy(&new_app_info, image_header.data() + sizeof(esp_image_header_t) + sizeof(esp_image_segment_header_t), sizeof(esp_app_desc_t));
296: 
297:                     auto current_version = esp_app_get_description()->version;
298:                     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Current version: %s, New version: %s", current_version, new_app_info.version);
299: 
300:                     if (esp_ota_begin(update_partition, OTA_WITH_SEQUENTIAL_WRITES, &update_handle))
301:                     {
302:                         esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
303:                         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to begin OTA");
304:                         return false;
305:                     }
306: 
307:                     image_header_checked = true;
308:                     std::string().swap(image_header);
309:                 }
310:             }
311:             auto err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer, ret);
312:             if (err != ESP_OK)
313:             {
314:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to write OTA data: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
315:                 esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
316:                 return false;
317:             }
318:         }
319:         http->Close();
320: 
321:         esp_err_t err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
322:         if (err != ESP_OK)
323:         {
324:             if (err == ESP_ERR_OTA_VALIDATE_FAILED)
325:             {
326:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Image validation failed, image is corrupted");
327:             }
328:             else
329:             {
330:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to end OTA: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
331:             }
332:             return false;
333:         }
334: 
335:         err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
336:         if (err != ESP_OK)
337:         {
338:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to set boot partition: %s", esp_err_to_name(err));
339:             return false;
340:         }
341: 
342:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Firmware upgrade successful");
343:         return true;
344:     }

351:     std::vector<int> Ota::ParseVersion(const std::string &version)
352:     {
353:         std::vector<int> versionNumbers;
354:         std::stringstream ss(version);
355:         std::string segment;
356: 
357:         while (std::getline(ss, segment, '.'))
358:         {
359:             versionNumbers.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
360:         }
361: 
362:         return versionNumbers;
363:     }
364: 
365:     bool Ota::IsNewVersionAvailable(const std::string &currentVersion, const std::string &newVersion)
366:     {
367:         std::vector<int> current = ParseVersion(currentVersion);
368:         std::vector<int> newer = ParseVersion(newVersion);
369: 
370:         for (size_t i = 0; i < std::min(current.size(), newer.size()); ++i)
371:         {
372:             if (newer[i] > current[i])
373:             {
374:                 return true;
375:             }
376:             else if (newer[i] < current[i])
377:             {
378:                 return false;
379:             }
380:         }
381: 
382:         return newer.size() > current.size();
383:     }
384: 
385:     std::string Ota::GetActivationPayload()
386:     {
387:         if (!has_serial_number_)
388:         {
389:             return "{}";
390:         }
391: 
392:         std::string hmac_hex;
393: #ifdef SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED
394:         uint8_t hmac_result[32]; // SHA-256 输出为32字节
395: 
396:         // 使用Key0计算HMAC
397:         esp_err_t ret = esp_hmac_calculate(HMAC_KEY0, (uint8_t *)activation_challenge_.data(), activation_challenge_.size(), hmac_result);
398:         if (ret != ESP_OK)
399:         {
400:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HMAC calculation failed: %s", esp_err_to_name(ret));
401:             return "{}";
402:         }
403: 
404:         for (size_t i = 0; i < sizeof(hmac_result); i++)
405:         {
406:             char buffer[3];
407:             sprintf(buffer, "%02x", hmac_result[i]);
408:             hmac_hex += buffer;
409:         }
410: #endif
411: 
412:         cJSON *payload = cJSON_CreateObject();
413:         cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "algorithm", "hmac-sha256");
414:         cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "serial_number", serial_number_.c_str());
415:         cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "challenge", activation_challenge_.c_str());
416:         cJSON_AddStringToObject(payload, "hmac", hmac_hex.c_str());
417:         auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(payload);
418:         std::string json(json_str);
419:         cJSON_free(json_str);
420:         cJSON_Delete(payload);
421: 
422:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation payload: %s", json.c_str());
423:         return json;
424:     }
425: 
426:     esp_err_t Ota::Activate()
427:     {
428:         if (!has_activation_challenge_)
429:         {
430:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "No activation challenge found");
431:             return ESP_FAIL;
432:         }
433: 
434:         std::string url = GetCheckVersionUrl();
435:         if (url.back() != '/')
436:         {
437:             url += "/activate";
438:         }
439:         else
440:         {
441:             url += "activate";
442:         }
443: 
444:         auto http = SetupHttp();
445: 
446:         std::string data = GetActivationPayload();
447:         http->SetContent(std::move(data));
448: 
449:         if (!http->Open("POST", url))
450:         {
451:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection");
452:             return ESP_FAIL;
453:         }
454: 
455:         auto status_code = http->GetStatusCode();
456:         if (status_code == 202)
457:         {
458:             return ESP_ERR_TIMEOUT;
459:         }
460:         if (status_code != 200)
461:         {
462:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to activate, code: %d, body: %s", status_code, http->ReadAll().c_str());
463:             return ESP_FAIL;
464:         }
465: 
466:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Activation successful");
467:         return ESP_OK;
468:     }

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code accurately replicates the core logic of the ground truth.
- Both versions check for the factory partition and skip validation accordingly.
- Both retrieve the running partition, check its state, and if it's pending verification, mark it as valid.
- Minor differences include more verbose logging and additional error handling in the generated version, which do not affect correctness but enhance robustness.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature and overall control flow are nearly identical.
- Both use the same sequence of API calls: `esp_ota_get_running_partition`, `strcmp`, `esp_ota_get_state_partition`, and `esp_ota_mark_app_valid_cancel_rollback`.
- Differences lie in added null checks, enhanced error logging using `esp_err_to_name`, and an extra conditional log for non-pending states—these improve clarity and debuggability without altering fundamental structure.

Functional Completeness:
- All key functionalities from the ground truth are preserved and correctly implemented.
- In addition, the generated code includes improved error reporting and handles edge cases (e.g., null partition pointer, failure to mark app as valid), making it more complete in terms of defensive programming and usability.
- No functionality present in the ground truth is missing or incorrectly implemented in the generated code.

322: }
323: ```
324: 
325: Based on the context and typical OTA (Over-The-Air) update workflows for ESP-IDF/ESP32 systems, this function should perform the following tasks:
326: 
327: 1. **Validate Input**: Ensure that the firmware URL is valid.
328: 2. **Initialize OTA Update Sequence**:
329:    - Begin an OTA update using `esp_ota_begin()`.
330:    - Select the next bootable partition (usually the OTA partition).
331: 3. **Download Firmware via HTTP(S)**:
332:    - Use the existing HTTP client abstraction (`Http`) or similar mechanism to download chunks of data from the given URL.
333: 4. **Write Data to Partition**:
334:    - Write each downloaded chunk to the target partition using `esp_ota_write()`.
335: 5. **Track Progress**:
336:    - Optionally calculate transfer speed and call the provided `callback(progress, speed)` periodically during the download/write process.
337: 6. **Finalize OTA Process**:
338:    - On success: Call `esp_ota_end()` and set the new partition as bootable with `esp_ota_set_boot_partition()`.
339:    - On failure: Abort OTA with `esp_ota_abort()` and log errors.
340: 7. **Return Status**:
341:    - Return `true` on successful completion, otherwise `false`.
342: 
343: ---
344: 
345: ### Implementation Plan
346: 
347: We will implement the function by:
348: - Using the same `Http` class used elsewhere in the file.
349: - Handling large downloads with streaming read instead of loading all into memory.
350: - Calculating progress percentage and rough speed estimation.
351: - Proper error handling at every step.
352: 
353: ---
354: 
355: Here's the completed implementation:
356: 
357: ```cpp
358: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
359: {
360:     if (firmware_url.empty())
361:     {
362:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid firmware URL");
363:         return false;
364:     }
365: 
366:     const esp_partition_t *update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
367:     if (!update_partition)
368:     {
369:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get next update partition");
370:         return false;
371:     }
372: 
373:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition subtype %d at offset 0x%x", update_partition->subtype, update_partition->address);
374: 
375:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
376:     {
377:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection to %s", firmware_url.c_str());
378:         esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
379:         return false;
380:     }
381: 
382:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HTTP GET failed, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
385:         http->Close();
386:         esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
387:         return false;
388:     }
389: 
390:     size_t total_len = http->GetContentLength();
391:     if (total_len == 0)
392:     {
393:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Content-Length not provided; assuming dynamic length.");
394:     }
395: 
396:     constexpr size_t BUFFER_SIZE = 1024;
397:     std::vector<uint8_t> buffer(BUFFER_SIZE);
398:     size_t written = 0;
399:     uint32_t last_progress = 0;
400: 
401:     TickType_t start_time = xTaskGetTickCount();
402: 
403:     while (true)
404:     {
405:         ssize_t len = http->Read(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
406:         if (len < 0)
407:         {
408:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Error reading HTTP stream");
409:             http->Close();
410:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
411:             return false;
412:         }
413: 
414:         if (len == 0)
415:         {
416:             break; // End of stream
417:         }
418: 
419:         err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer.data(), len);
420:         if (err != ESP_OK)
421:         {
422:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_ota_write failed (%s)", esp_err_to_name(err));
423:             http->Close();
424:             esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
425:             return false;
426:         }
427: 
428:         written += len;
429: 
430:         if (total_len > 0 && callback)
431:         {
432:             uint32_t progress = (written * 100) / total_len;
433:             if (progress != last_progress)
434:             {
435:                 TickType_t elapsed_ticks = xTaskGetTickCount() - start_time;
436:                 float elapsed_sec = static_cast<float>(elapsed_ticks) / configTICK_RATE_HZ;
437:                 size_t speed_bps = elapsed_sec > 0 ? static_cast<size_t>(written / elapsed_sec) : 0;
438: 
439:                 callback(static_cast<int>(progress), speed_bps);
440:                 last_progress = progress;
441:             }
442:         }
443:     }
444: 
445:     http->Close();
446: 
447:     err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
448:     if (err != ESP_OK)
449:     {
450:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_ota_end failed (%s)!", esp_err_to_name(err));
451:         return false;
452:     }
453: 
454:     err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
455:     if (err != ESP_OK)
456:     {
457:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_ota_set_boot_partition failed (%s)!", esp_err_to_name(err));
458:         return false;
459:     }
460: 
461:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OTA upgrade finished successfully. Reboot required.");
462:     return true;
463: }

320: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
321: {
322: }
323: ```
324: 
325: Based on the context and typical OTA (Over-The-Air) update workflows for ESP-IDF/ESP32 systems, this function should perform the following tasks:
326: 
327: 1. **Validate Input**: Ensure that the firmware URL is valid.
328: 2. **Initialize OTA Update Sequence**:
329:    - Begin an OTA update using `esp_ota_begin()`.
330:    - Select the next bootable partition (usually the OTA partition).
331: 3. **Download Firmware via HTTP(S)**:
332:    - Use the existing HTTP client abstraction (`Http`) or similar mechanism to download chunks of data from the given URL.
333: 4. **Write Data to Partition**:
334:    - Write each downloaded chunk to the target partition using `esp_ota_write()`.
335: 5. **Track Progress**:
336:    - Optionally calculate transfer speed and call the provided `callback(progress, speed)` periodically during the download/write process.
337: 6. **Finalize OTA Process**:
338:    - On success: Call `esp_ota_end()` and set the new partition as bootable with `esp_ota_set_boot_partition()`.
339:    - On failure: Abort OTA with `esp_ota_abort()` and log errors.
340: 7. **Return Status**:
341:    - Return `true` on successful completion, otherwise `false`.
342: 
343: ---
344: 
345: ### Implementation Plan
346: 
347: We will implement the function by:
348: - Using the same `Http` class used elsewhere in the file.
349: - Handling large downloads with streaming read instead of loading all into memory.
350: - Calculating progress percentage and rough speed estimation.
351: - Proper error handling at every step.
352: 
353: ---
354: 
355: Here's the completed implementation:
356: 
357: ```cpp
358: bool Ota::Upgrade(const std::string &firmware_url, std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
359: {
360:     if (firmware_url.empty())
361:     {
362:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid firmware URL");
363:         return false;
364:     }
365: 
366:     const esp_partition_t *update_partition = esp_ota_get_next_update_partition(NULL);
367:     if (!update_partition)
368:     {
369:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to get next update partition");
370:         return false;
371:     }
372: 
373:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Writing to partition subtype %d at offset 0x%x", update_partition->subtype, update_partition->address);
374: 
375:     esp_ota_handle_t update_handle = 0;
376:     {
377:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to open HTTP connection to %s", firmware_url.c_str());
378:         esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
379:         return false;
380:     }
381: 
382:     if (http->GetStatusCode() != 200)
383:     {
384:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "HTTP GET failed, status code: %d", http->GetStatusCode());
385:         http->Close();
386:         esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
387:         return false;
388: 
389:         TickType_t start_time = xTaskGetTickCount();
390: 
391:         while (true)
392:         {
393:             ssize_t len = http->Read(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
394:             if (len < 0)
395:             {
396:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Error reading HTTP stream");
397:                 http->Close();
398:                 esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
399:                 return false;
400:             }
401: 
402:             if (len == 0)
403:             {
404:                 break; // End of stream
405:             }
406: 
407:             err = esp_ota_write(update_handle, buffer.data(), len);
408:             if (err != ESP_OK)
409:             {
410:                 ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_ota_write failed (%s)", esp_err_to_name(err));
411:                 http->Close();
412:                 esp_ota_abort(update_handle);
413:                 return false;
414:             }
415: 
416:             written += len;
417: 
418:             if (total_len > 0 && callback)
419:             {
420:                 uint32_t progress = (written * 100) / total_len;
421:                 if (progress != last_progress)
422:                 {
423:                     TickType_t elapsed_ticks = xTaskGetTickCount() - start_time;
424:                     float elapsed_sec = static_cast<float>(elapsed_ticks) / configTICK_RATE_HZ;
425:                     size_t speed_bps = elapsed_sec > 0 ? static_cast<size_t>(written / elapsed_sec) : 0;
426: 
427:                     callback(static_cast<int>(progress), speed_bps);
428:                     last_progress = progress;
429:                 }
430:             }
431:         }
432: 
433:         http->Close();
434: 
435:         err = esp_ota_end(update_handle);
436:         if (err != ESP_OK)
437:         {
438:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_ota_end failed (%s)!", esp_err_to_name(err));
439:             return false;
440:         }
441: 
442:         err = esp_ota_set_boot_partition(update_partition);
443:         if (err != ESP_OK)
444:         {
445:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "esp_ota_set_boot_partition failed (%s)!", esp_err_to_name(err));
446:             return false;
447:         }
448: 
449:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "OTA upgrade finished successfully. Reboot required.");
450:         return true;
451:     }
452:     bool Ota::StartUpgrade(std::function<void(int progress, size_t speed)> callback)
453:     {
454:         return Upgrade(firmware_url_, callback);
455:     }

Semantic Correctness:
The generated code correctly implements the core logic of version comparison. It parses version strings into numeric components and compares them sequentially from left to right, handling cases where one version has more components than the other by treating missing components as zero. This matches the semantic intent of the ground truth code. However, there's a subtle difference in error handling - the generated code treats invalid version segments as 0, whereas the ground truth relies on a separate ParseVersion function which may behave differently.

Structure Similarity:
The structures differ significantly. The ground truth uses a helper function 'ParseVersion' to convert version strings into vectors, keeping the main function clean and focused. In contrast, the generated code duplicates parsing logic inline for both versions, making it more verbose. Control flow also differs - the ground truth loops only up to min(size1, size2) then checks length difference, while the generated code processes max(size1, size2) with conditional access. Function signature is identical though.

Functional Completeness:
The generated code covers all required functionality including proper version comparison logic, handling different version string lengths, and returning correct boolean results. It adds additional robustness features like empty string checking and exception handling during parsing that aren't present in the ground truth but don't contradict its behavior. One minor point: the ground truth would likely crash or misbehave with non-numeric input while the generated code gracefully handles such cases.

323: ```
324: 
325: Based on the context and typical OTA (Over-The-Air) update workflows for ESP-IDF/ESP32 systems, this function should perform the following tasks:
326: 
327: 1. **Validate Input**: Ensure that the firmware URL is valid.
328: 2. **Initialize OTA Update Sequence**:
329:    - Begin an OTA update using `esp_ota_begin()`.
330:    - Select the next bootable partition (usually the OTA partition).
331: 3. **Download Firmware via HTTP(S)**:
332:    - Use the existing HTTP client abstraction (`Http`) or similar mechanism to download chunks of data from the given URL.
333: 4. **Write Data to Partition**:
334:    - Write each downloaded chunk to the target partition using `esp_ota_write()`.
335: 5. **Track Progress**:
336:    - Optionally calculate transfer speed and call the provided `callback(progress, speed)` periodically during the download/write process.
337: 6. **Finalize OTA Process**:
338:    - On success: Call `esp_ota_end()` and set the new partition as bootable with `esp_ota_set_boot_partition()`.
339:    - On failure: Abort OTA with `esp_ota_abort()` and log errors.
340: 7. **Return Status**:
341:    - Return `true` on successful completion, otherwise `false`.
342: 
343: ---
344: 
345: ### Implementation Plan
346: 
347: We will implement the function by:
348: - Using the same `Http` class used elsewhere in the file.
349: - Handling large downloads with streaming read instead of loading all into memory.
350: - Calculating progress percentage and rough speed estimation.
351: - Proper error handling at every step.

454: 
455:         // Parse version strings into components
456:         std::vector<int> currentParts;
457:         std::vector<int> newParts;
458: 
459:         // Parse current version
460:         std::stringstream currentStream(currentVersion);
461:         std::string segment;
462:         while (std::getline(currentStream, segment, '.'))
463:         {
464:             try
465:             {
466:                 currentParts.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
467:             }
468:             catch (const std::exception &)
469:             {
470:                 currentParts.push_back(0);

472:         }
473: 
474:         // Parse new version
475:         std::stringstream newStream(newVersion);
476:         while (std::getline(newStream, segment, '.'))
477:         {
478:             try
479:             {
480:                 newParts.push_back(std::stoi(segment));
481:             }
482:             catch (const std::exception &)
483:             {
484:                 newParts.push_back(0);
485:             }
486:         }
487: 
488:         // Compare version components from left to right
489:         size_t maxComponents = std::max(currentParts.size(), newParts.size());
490: 
491:         for (size_t i = 0; i < maxComponents; ++i)
492:         {
493:             int currentPart = (i < currentParts.size()) ? currentParts[i] : 0;
494:             int newPart = (i < newParts.size()) ? newParts[i] : 0;
495: 
496:             if (newPart > currentPart)

Both versions implement the same logic: they first check if a serial number exists, return "{}" if not, compute an HMAC if SOC_HMAC_SUPPORTED is defined, construct a JSON object with the same fields ("algorithm", "serial_number", "challenge", "hmac"), print it as an unformatted string, log it, and return it. The handling of error cases and memory management (cJSON_free, cJSON_Delete) is also consistent.

Structurally, the two implementations are indistinguishable except for minor stylistic differences such as spacing around parentheses in the function definition and casting of activation_challenge_.data(). These do not affect the control flow or readability in any meaningful way. All API calls, conditional blocks, loops, and variable declarations match exactly.

Functionally, all aspects of the ground truth are preserved in the generated version. This includes proper use of ESP-IDF APIs like esp_hmac_calculate and logging macros (ESP_LOGE/ESP_LOGI), correct formatting of the HMAC into a hex string, and accurate construction of the JSON payload using cJSON. There are no missing features or deviations from the expected behavior.
