qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness: The generated code correctly implements the core Goertzel algorithm logic, computing the new state value based on the difference equation and updating the buffer accordingly. However, it assumes a fixed-size buffer accessed via indexing, whereas the ground truth uses a deque-like structure with push/pop operations. This leads to slightly different semantics in terms of how the buffer is managed.

Structure Similarity: Both versions follow the same high-level approach of implementing the Goertzel recurrence relation. However, the control flow differs significantly—ground truth includes an early return for small buffers and explicitly manipulates a deque, while the generated code assumes a pre-initialized two-element array. Function signature matches exactly.

Functional Completeness: The generated code misses the safeguard present in the ground truth that checks if the state buffer has at least two elements before proceeding. It also doesn't replicate the exact sequence of push/pop operations which might be important depending on how `state_buffer_` is used elsewhere. Thus, some edge-case handling and buffer management functionality from the ground truth is missing.

189:         : input_buffer_size_(window_size), output_sample_count_(0)
190:     {

193:             // On ESP32 we can continue execution, but log the error

201:         space_detector_ = std::make_unique<FrequencyDetector>(normalized_space_freq, window_size);
202: 
203:         samples_per_bit_ = sample_rate / bit_rate; // Number of samples per bit
204:     }

207:     {
208:         std::vector<float> result;
209: 
210:         for (float sample : samples)
211:         {
212:             if (input_buffer_.size() < input_buffer_size_)

215:             }
216:             else

221:                 output_sample_count_++;
222: 
223:                 if (output_sample_count_ >= samples_per_bit_)
224:                 {

226:                     for (float window_sample : input_buffer_)

234:

237:                                              (space_amplitude + mark_amplitude + std::numeric_limits<float>::epsilon());

294:     {

305:             // Process received bit based on state machine

312:                     ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Waiting state");

342:                         uint8_t received_checksum = 0;

348:                             minimum_length = 1 + start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
349:                             if (bytes.size() >= minimum_length)

351:                                 received_checksum = bytes[bytes.size() - start_of_transmission_.size() / 8 - 1];
352:                             }
353:                         }
354:                         else
355:                         {
356:                             minimum_length = start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
357:                         }
358: 
359:                         if (bytes.size() < minimum_length)
360:                         {
361:                             ClearBuffers();
362:                             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Data too short, clearing buffer");
363:                             return false; // Data too short, return failure
364:                         }
365: 
366:                         // Extract text data (remove trailing identifier part)
367:                         std::vector<uint8_t> text_bytes(
368:                             bytes.begin(), bytes.begin() + bytes.size() - minimum_length);
369: 
370:                         std::string result(text_bytes.begin(), text_bytes.end());
371: 
372:                         // Validate checksum if required
373:                         if (enable_checksum_validation_)
374:                         {
375:                             uint8_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(result);
376:                             if (calculated_checksum != received_checksum)
377:                             {
378:                                 // Checksum mismatch
379:                                 ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Checksum mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
380:                                          received_checksum, calculated_checksum);
381:                                 ClearBuffers();
382:                                 return false;
383:                             }
384:                         }
385: 
386:                         ClearBuffers();
387:                         decoded_text = result;
388:                         return true; // Return success
389:                     }
390:                     else if (bit_buffer_.size() >= max_bit_buffer_size_)
391:                     {
392:                         // If not end identifier and bit buffer is full, reset
393:                         ClearBuffers();
394:                         ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Buffer overflow, clearing buffer");
395:                         current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Reset state machine
396:                     }
397:                 }
398:                 break;
399:             }
400:         }
401: 
402:         return false;
403:     }
404: 
405:     std::vector<uint8_t> AudioDataBuffer::ConvertBitsToBytes(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bits) const
406:     {
407:         std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
408: 
409:         // Ensure number of bits is a multiple of 8
410:         size_t complete_bytes_count = bits.size() / 8;
411:         bytes.reserve(complete_bytes_count);
412: 
413:         for (size_t i = 0; i < complete_bytes_count; ++i)
414:         {
415:             uint8_t byte_value = 0;
416:             for (size_t j = 0; j < 8; ++j)
417:             {
418:                 byte_value |= bits[i * 8 + j] << (7 - j);
419:             }
420:             bytes.push_back(byte_value);
421:         }
422: 
423:         return bytes;
424:     }
425: }

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code diverges significantly in semantic meaning from the ground truth due to fundamental differences in how audio samples are processed:

Semantic Issues:
- The generated code processes every incoming sample immediately with both detectors, while the ground truth buffers samples until a sufficient window (input_buffer_size_) is reached before processing.
- In the ground truth, processing involves a sliding window mechanism where old samples are removed as new ones come in (using pop_front()), which is entirely missing in the generated version.
- The Goertzel algorithm application differs semantically — in ground truth it's applied over all buffered samples within a window, whereas in the generated code each sample is fed individually without context of the required detection window.

Structural Differences:
- Function signature matches but internal logic and variable usage differ significantly.
- Control flow lacks buffering logic and conditional checks for buffer state found in the reference implementation.
- Missing use of member variables such as input_buffer_, input_buffer_size_ that play a critical role in the correct functioning per the ground truth.

Functional Gaps:
- No buffering mechanism or fixed-size window management.
- Lacks proper synchronization between input buffering and output generation based on samples_per_bit_ relative to the maintained window.
- Critical components like maintaining an ordered history of samples via deque operations (pop_front(), push_back()) are absent.

While the generated code attempts to compute probabilities similarly using amplitude ratios, it omits essential preconditions and architectural elements necessary for accurate signal demodulation. This results in incorrect timing and contextual analysis of the audio stream.

271:                 {
272:                     for (float probability : probabilities)
273:                     {

275: 
276:                         if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= identifier_buffer_size_)
277:                         {
278:                             identifier_buffer_.pop_front(); // Maintain buffer size
279:                         }
280:                         identifier_buffer_.push_back(bit);
281: 
282:                         // Process received bit based on state machine
283:                         switch (current_state_)
284:                         {
285:                         case DataReceptionState::kInactive:
286:                             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
287:                             {
288:                                 current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kWaiting; // Enter waiting state
289:                                 ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Waiting state");
290:                             }
291:                             break;
292: 
293:                         case DataReceptionState::kWaiting:
294:                             // Waiting state, possibly waiting for transmission end
295:                             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
296:                             {
297:                                 std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
298:                                 if (identifier_snapshot == start_of_transmission_)
299:                                 {
300:                                     ClearBuffers();                                  // Clear buffers
301:                                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kReceiving; // Enter receiving state
302:                                     ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Receiving state");
303:                                 }
304:                             }
305:                             break;
306: 
307:                         case DataReceptionState::kReceiving:
308:                             bit_buffer_.push_back(bit);
309:                             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= end_of_transmission_.size())
310:                             {
311:                                 std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
312:                                 if (identifier_snapshot == end_of_transmission_)
313:                                 {
314:                                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Enter inactive state
315: 
316:                                     // Convert bits to bytes
317:                                     std::vector<uint8_t> bytes = ConvertBitsToBytes(bit_buffer_);

319:                                     uint8_t received_checksum = 0;
320:                                     size_t minimum_length = 0;
321: 
322:                                     if (enable_checksum_validation_)
323:                                     {
324:                                         // If checksum is required, last byte is checksum
325:                                         minimum_length = 1 + start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
326:                                         if (bytes.size() >= minimum_length)
327:                                         {
328:                                             received_checksum = bytes[bytes.size() - start_of_transmission_.size() / 8 - 1];
329:                                         }
330:                                     }
331:                                     else
332:                                     {
333:                                         minimum_length = start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
334:                                     }
335: 
336:                                     if (bytes.size() < minimum_length)
337:                                     {
338:                                         ClearBuffers();
339:                                         ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Data too short, clearing buffer");
340:                                         return false; // Data too short, return failure
341:                                     }
342: 
343:                                     // Extract text data (remove trailing identifier part)
344:                                     std::vector<uint8_t> text_bytes(
345:                                         bytes.begin(), bytes.begin() + bytes.size() - minimum_length);
346: 
347:                                     std::string result(text_bytes.begin(), text_bytes.end());
348: 
349:                                     // Validate checksum if required
350:                                     if (enable_checksum_validation_)
351:                                     {
352:                                         uint8_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(result);
353:                                         if (calculated_checksum != received_checksum)
354:                                         {
355:                                             // Checksum mismatch
356:                                             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Checksum mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
357:                                                      received_checksum, calculated_checksum);
358:                                             ClearBuffers();
359:                                             return false;
360:                                         }
361:                                     }
362: 
363:                                     ClearBuffers();
364:                                     decoded_text = result;
365:                                     return true; // Return success
366:                                 }
367:                                 else if (bit_buffer_.size() >= max_bit_buffer_size_)
368:                                 {
369:                                     // If not end identifier and bit buffer is full, reset
370:                                     ClearBuffers();
371:                                     ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Buffer overflow, clearing buffer");
372:                                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Reset state machine
373:                                 }
374:                             }
375:                             break;
376:                         }
377:                     }
378: 
379:                     return false;
380:                 }
381: 
382:                 std::vector<uint8_t> AudioDataBuffer::ConvertBitsToBytes(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bits) const
383:                 {
384:                     std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
385: 
386:                     // Ensure number of bits is a multiple of 8
387:                     size_t complete_bytes_count = bits.size() / 8;
388:                     bytes.reserve(complete_bytes_count);
389: 
390:                     for (size_t i = 0; i < complete_bytes_count; ++i)
391:                     {
392:                         uint8_t byte_value = 0;
393:                         for (size_t j = 0; j < 8; ++j)
394:                         {
395:                             byte_value |= bits[i * 8 + j] << (7 - j);
396:                         }
397:                         bytes.push_back(byte_value);
398:                     }
399: 
400:                     return bytes;
401:                 }
402:             }

The additional overloaded constructor also semantically aligns with typical C++ practices for configurable object construction.

This addition was not part of the original task or ground truth but doesn't detract from correctness. Stylistically, both versions are consistent, using initializer lists appropriately and organizing member initializations similarly.

However, since the task only required completing `AudioDataBuffer()`, the second constructor could be seen as over-engineering or out-of-scope functionality.

133:     // FrequencyDetector implementation
134:     FrequencyDetector::FrequencyDetector(float frequency, size_t window_size)
135:         : frequency_(frequency), window_size_(window_size)
136:     {
137:         frequency_bin_ = std::floor(frequency_ * static_cast<float>(window_size_));
138:         angular_frequency_ = 2.0f * M_PI * frequency_;
139:         cos_coefficient_ = std::cos(angular_frequency_);
140:         sin_coefficient_ = std::sin(angular_frequency_);
141:         filter_coefficient_ = 2.0f * cos_coefficient_;
142: 
143:         // Initialize state buffer
144:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
145:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
146:     }
147: 
148:     void FrequencyDetector::Reset()
149:     {
150:         state_buffer_.clear();
151:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
152:         state_buffer_.push_back(0.0f);
153:     }
154: 
155:     void FrequencyDetector::ProcessSample(float sample)
156:     {
157:         if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
158:         {
159:             return;
160:         }
161: 
162:         float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-2]
163:         state_buffer_.pop_front();
164:         float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_.front(); // S[-1]
165:         state_buffer_.pop_front();
166: 
167:         float s_current = sample + filter_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2;
168: 
169:         state_buffer_.push_back(s_minus_1); // Put S[-1] back
170:         state_buffer_.push_back(s_current); // Add new S[0]
171:     }
172: 
173:     float FrequencyDetector::GetAmplitude() const
174:     {
175:         if (state_buffer_.size() < 2)
176:         {
177:             return 0.0f;
178:         }
179: 
180:         float s_minus_1 = state_buffer_[1];                         // S[-1]
181:         float s_minus_2 = state_buffer_[0];                         // S[-2]
182:         float real_part = cos_coefficient_ * s_minus_1 - s_minus_2; // Real part
183:         float imaginary_part = sin_coefficient_ * s_minus_1;        // Imaginary part
184: 
185:         return std::sqrt(real_part * real_part + imaginary_part * imaginary_part) /
186:                (static_cast<float>(window_size_) / 2.0f);
187:     }
188: 
189:     // AudioSignalProcessor implementation
190:     AudioSignalProcessor::AudioSignalProcessor(size_t sample_rate, size_t mark_frequency, size_t space_frequency,
191:                                                size_t bit_rate, size_t window_size)
192:         : input_buffer_size_(window_size), output_sample_count_(0)
193:     {
194:         if (sample_rate % bit_rate != 0)
195:         {
196:             // On ESP32 we can continue execution, but log the error
197:             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Sample rate %zu is not divisible by bit rate %zu", sample_rate, bit_rate);
198:         }
199: 
200:         float normalized_mark_freq = static_cast<float>(mark_frequency) / static_cast<float>(sample_rate);
201:         float normalized_space_freq = static_cast<float>(space_frequency) / static_cast<float>(sample_rate);
202: 
203:         mark_detector_ = std::make_unique<FrequencyDetector>(normalized_mark_freq, window_size);
204:         space_detector_ = std::make_unique<FrequencyDetector>(normalized_space_freq, window_size);
205: 
206:         samples_per_bit_ = sample_rate / bit_rate; // Number of samples per bit
207:     }
208: 
209:     std::vector<float> AudioSignalProcessor::ProcessAudioSamples(const std::vector<float> &samples)
210:     {
211:         std::vector<float> result;
212: 
213:         for (float sample : samples)
214:         {
215:             if (input_buffer_.size() < input_buffer_size_)
216:             {
217:                 input_buffer_.push_back(sample); // Just add, don't process yet
218:             }
219:             else
220:             {
221:                 // Input buffer is full, process the data
222:                 input_buffer_.pop_front();       // Remove oldest sample
223:                 input_buffer_.push_back(sample); // Add new sample
224:                 output_sample_count_++;
225: 
226:                 if (output_sample_count_ >= samples_per_bit_)
227:                 {
228:                     // Process all samples in the window using Goertzel algorithm
229:                     for (float window_sample : input_buffer_)
230:                     {
231:                         mark_detector_->ProcessSample(window_sample);
232:                         space_detector_->ProcessSample(window_sample);
233:                     }
234: 
235:                     float mark_amplitude = mark_detector_->GetAmplitude();   // Mark amplitude
236:                     float space_amplitude = space_detector_->GetAmplitude(); // Space amplitude
237: 
238:                     // Avoid division by zero
239:                     float mark_probability = mark_amplitude /
240:                                              (space_amplitude + mark_amplitude + std::numeric_limits<float>::epsilon());
241:                     result.push_back(mark_probability);
242: 
243:                     // Reset detector windows
244:                     mark_detector_->Reset();
245:                     space_detector_->Reset();
246:                     output_sample_count_ = 0; // Reset output counter
247:                 }
248:             }
249:         }
250: 
251:         return result;
252:     }
253: 
254:     // AudioDataBuffer implementation
255:     AudioDataBuffer::AudioDataBuffer()

262:         max_bit_buffer_size_ = 776; // Preset bit buffer size, 776 bits = (32 + 1 + 63 + 1) * 8 = 776
263: 
264:         bit_buffer_.reserve(max_bit_buffer_size_);

267:     AudioDataBuffer::AudioDataBuffer(size_t max_byte_size, const std::vector<uint8_t> &start_identifier,
268:                                      const std::vector<uint8_t> &end_identifier, bool enable_checksum)
269:         : current_state_(DataReceptionState::kInactive),

272:           enable_checksum_validation_(enable_checksum)

275:         max_bit_buffer_size_ = max_byte_size * 8; // Bit buffer size in bytes

280:     uint8_t AudioDataBuffer::CalculateChecksum(const std::string &text)
281:     {

283:         for (char character : text)

285:             checksum += static_cast<uint8_t>(character);
286:         }
287:         return checksum;
288:     }

290:     void AudioDataBuffer::ClearBuffers()
291:     {
292:     }

294:     bool AudioDataBuffer::ProcessProbabilityData(const std::vector<float> &probabilities, float threshold)
295:     {
296:         for (float probability : probabilities)
297:         {
298:             uint8_t bit = (probability > threshold) ? 1 : 0;

300:             if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= identifier_buffer_size_)
301:             {
302:                 identifier_buffer_.pop_front(); // Maintain buffer size
303:             }
304:             identifier_buffer_.push_back(bit);
305: 
306:             // Process received bit based on state machine
307:             switch (current_state_)
308:             {
309:             case DataReceptionState::kInactive:
310:                 if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
311:                 {
312:                     current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kWaiting; // Enter waiting state
313:                     ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Waiting state");
314:                 }
315:                 break;
316: 
317:             case DataReceptionState::kWaiting:
318:                 // Waiting state, possibly waiting for transmission end
319:                 if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= start_of_transmission_.size())
320:                 {
321:                     std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
322:                     if (identifier_snapshot == start_of_transmission_)
323:                     {
324:                         ClearBuffers();                                  // Clear buffers
325:                         current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kReceiving; // Enter receiving state
326:                         ESP_LOGI(kLogTag, "Entering Receiving state");
327:                     }
328:                 }
329:                 break;
330: 
331:             case DataReceptionState::kReceiving:
332:                 bit_buffer_.push_back(bit);
333:                 if (identifier_buffer_.size() >= end_of_transmission_.size())
334:                 {
335:                     std::vector<uint8_t> identifier_snapshot(identifier_buffer_.begin(), identifier_buffer_.end());
336:                     if (identifier_snapshot == end_of_transmission_)
337:                     {
338:                         current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Enter inactive state
339: 
340:                         // Convert bits to bytes
341:                         std::vector<uint8_t> bytes = ConvertBitsToBytes(bit_buffer_);
342: 
343:                         uint8_t received_checksum = 0;
344:                         size_t minimum_length = 0;
345: 
346:                         if (enable_checksum_validation_)
347:                         {
348:                             // If checksum is required, last byte is checksum
349:                             minimum_length = 1 + start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
350:                             if (bytes.size() >= minimum_length)
351:                             {
352:                                 received_checksum = bytes[bytes.size() - start_of_transmission_.size() / 8 - 1];
353:                             }
354:                         }
355:                         else
356:                         {
357:                             minimum_length = start_of_transmission_.size() / 8;
358:                         }
359: 
360:                         if (bytes.size() < minimum_length)
361:                         {
362:                             ClearBuffers();
363:                             ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Data too short, clearing buffer");
364:                             return false; // Data too short, return failure
365:                         }
366: 
367:                         // Extract text data (remove trailing identifier part)
368:                         std::vector<uint8_t> text_bytes(
369:                             bytes.begin(), bytes.begin() + bytes.size() - minimum_length);
370: 
371:                         std::string result(text_bytes.begin(), text_bytes.end());
372: 
373:                         // Validate checksum if required
374:                         if (enable_checksum_validation_)
375:                         {
376:                             uint8_t calculated_checksum = CalculateChecksum(result);
377:                             if (calculated_checksum != received_checksum)
378:                             {
379:                                 // Checksum mismatch
380:                                 ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Checksum mismatch: expected %d, got %d",
381:                                          received_checksum, calculated_checksum);
382:                                 ClearBuffers();
383:                                 return false;
384:                             }
385:                         }
386: 
387:                         ClearBuffers();
388:                         decoded_text = result;
389:                         return true; // Return success
390:                     }
391:                     else if (bit_buffer_.size() >= max_bit_buffer_size_)
392:                     {
393:                         // If not end identifier and bit buffer is full, reset
394:                         ClearBuffers();
395:                         ESP_LOGW(kLogTag, "Buffer overflow, clearing buffer");
396:                         current_state_ = DataReceptionState::kInactive; // Reset state machine
397:                     }
398:                 }
399:                 break;
400:             }
401:         }
402: 
403:         return false;
404:     }
405: 
406:     std::vector<uint8_t> AudioDataBuffer::ConvertBitsToBytes(const std::vector<uint8_t> &bits) const
407:     {
408:         std::vector<uint8_t> bytes;
409: 
410:         // Ensure number of bits is a multiple of 8
411:         size_t complete_bytes_count = bits.size() / 8;
412:         bytes.reserve(complete_bytes_count);
413: 
414:         for (size_t i = 0; i < complete_bytes_count; ++i)
415:         {
416:             uint8_t byte_value = 0;
417:             for (size_t j = 0; j < 8; ++j)
418:             {
419:                 byte_value |= bits[i * 8 + j] << (7 - j);
420:             }
421:             bytes.push_back(byte_value);
422:         }
423: 
424:         return bytes;
425:     }
426: }

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to implement a similar state-based processing mechanism for audio data decoding but introduces several semantic deviations:
- Uses >= instead of > when comparing probability to threshold, changing behavior slightly.
- Combines kInactive and kWaiting states into one case, missing the explicit waiting state transition logic.
- Incorrectly clears identifier_buffer_ upon detecting start transmission which breaks subsequent end detection.
- Handles checksum validation differently by treating it as part of string rather than separate byte.
- Removes trailing bits incorrectly by popping from bit_buffer_ rather than slicing properly.

Structure Similarity:
The overall structure diverges significantly:
- Merges multiple states into fewer switch cases.
- Uses std::equal directly instead of creating snapshots and comparing vectors.
- Different handling of buffer maintenance (identifier_buffer_) with inconsistent size management.
- Lacks proper logging messages that indicate specific state transitions like "Entering Receiving state".
- Function signature matches, but internal control flow is restructured substantially.

Functional Completeness:
Covers core functionality but misses or alters important aspects:
- Implements basic bit conversion and buffering.
- Detects start/end patterns using equality checks.
- Converts bits to bytes and handles simple checksum validation.
- Missing detailed error handling paths such as minimum length checks and precise buffer overflow responses.
- Lacks clear separation between waiting and receiving states which affects reliability.
- Does not replicate exact sequence of operations around checksum extraction and validation.
- Some edge cases in original (like maintaining fixed-size identifier buffer) are not preserved accurately.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (60/100):
The core functionality differs significantly in bit ordering. Button::~Button()

```

In an embedded system using heap-allocated resources or handles (like `button_handle_`), it's important that any allocated resources are properly released when the object goes out of scope. In this case:

- The `button_handle_` represents a handle to a button device created via functions like `iot_button_new_gpio_device()` or `iot_button_new_adc_device()`.
- These devices should be deleted when no longer needed, likely through a corresponding cleanup function such as `iot_button_delete()`.

Therefore, the destructor should call the appropriate cleanup function if `button_handle_` is valid.

---

### Implementation Plan

To complete the `~Button()` function:
1. Check whether `button_handle_` is not null.
2. If valid, call the appropriate API to delete/free the button resource.
3. Based on common patterns in ESP-IDF style drivers, assume there exists a function like `iot_button_delete(button_handle_)`.

---

### Final Code

Here is the completed destructor implementation:

```cpp
Button::~Button()
{
    if (button_handle_ != nullptr) {
        iot_button_delete(button_handle_);
    }
}

324: std::string WifiBoard::GetDeviceStatusJson()
325: {
326:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
327:     auto root = cJSON_CreateObject();
328: 
329:     // Audio speaker
330:     auto audio_speaker = cJSON_CreateObject();
331:     if (auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec())
332:     {
333:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_speaker, "volume", codec->output_volume());
334:     }
335:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_speaker", audio_speaker);
336: 
337:     // Screen
338:     auto screen = cJSON_CreateObject();
339:     if (auto backlight = board.GetBacklight())
340:     {
341:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(screen, "brightness", backlight->brightness());
342:     }
343:     if (auto display = board.GetDisplay(); display && display->height() > 64)
344:     {
345:         if (auto theme = display->GetTheme())
346:         {
347:             cJSON_AddStringToObject(screen, "theme", theme->name().c_str());
348:         }
349:     }
350:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "screen", screen);
351: 
352:     // Battery
353:     int level = 0;
354:     bool charging = false, discharging = false;
355:     if (board.GetBatteryLevel(level, charging, discharging))
356:     {
357:         auto battery = cJSON_CreateObject();
358:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(battery, "level", level);
359:         cJSON_AddBoolToObject(battery, "charging", charging);
360:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "battery", battery);
361:     }
362: 
363:     // Network
364:     auto &wifi = WifiManager::GetInstance();
365:     auto network = cJSON_CreateObject();
366:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "type", "wifi");
367:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "ssid", wifi.GetSsid().c_str());
368:     int rssi = wifi.GetRssi();
369:     const char *signal = rssi >= -60 ? "strong" : (rssi >= -70 ? "medium" : "weak");
370:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "signal", signal);
371:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "network", network);
372: 
373:     // Chip temperature
374:     float temp = 0.0f;
375:     if (board.GetTemperature(temp))
376:     {
377:         auto chip = cJSON_CreateObject();
378:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(chip, "temperature", temp);
379:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "chip", chip);
380:     }
381: 
382:     auto str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
383:     std::string result(str);
384:     cJSON_free(str);
385:     cJSON_Delete(root);
386:     return result;
387: }

</file>

The control flow patterns are entirely different - ground truth has conditional checks and API calls while generated code has none.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
The generated code attempts to start a WiFi network and register an event handler, which aligns with the overall purpose of the ground truth. However, there are significant semantic differences:
- It uses `SetNetworkEventHandler` instead of `SetEventCallback`, suggesting a different mechanism for handling events.
- The lambda captures only `this`, whereas the ground truth captures both `this` and `wifi_manager`.
- There's no initialization of the WiFi manager with configuration (`ssid_prefix`, `language`) as seen in the ground truth.
- The mapping between `WifiEvent` and `NetworkEvent` is missing entirely in the generated version.

Structure Similarity:
Structurally, some elements match such as obtaining the singleton instance of `WifiManager` and calling `TryWifiConnect()`. However:
- Function calls differ significantly — e.g., `SetNetworkEventHandler` vs `SetEventCallback` + `Initialize`.
- Logging via `ESP_LOGI` is present in the generated code but not in the ground truth.
- Control flow lacks the detailed switch-case logic that maps internal WiFi events to external network events.

Functional Completeness:
Several critical functionalities from the ground truth are absent in the generated code:
- No initialization of the WiFi manager with specific configurations (SSID prefix, language).
- Missing event translation layer where `WifiEvent`s are mapped to `NetworkEvent`s.
- Absence of support for multiple event types like Scanning, Connecting, Connected, Disconnected, ConfigModeEnter/Exit.
- No retrieval or usage of SSID during event processing.
Overall, while basic connectivity setup is attempted, essential features required by the interface or system behavior are omitted.

294: {

313:     {

315:         {
316:             cJSON_AddStringToObject(screen, "theme", theme->name().c_str());
317:         }
318:     }
319:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "screen", screen);
320: 
321:     // Battery
322:     int level = 0;
323:     bool charging = false, discharging = false;
324:     if (board.GetBatteryLevel(level, charging, discharging))
325:     {
326:         auto battery = cJSON_CreateObject();
327:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(battery, "level", level);
328:         cJSON_AddBoolToObject(battery, "charging", charging);
329:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "battery", battery);
330:     }
331: 
332:     // Network
333:     auto &wifi = WifiManager::GetInstance();
334:     auto network = cJSON_CreateObject();
335:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "type", "wifi");
336:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "ssid", wifi.GetSsid().c_str());
337:     int rssi = wifi.GetRssi();

339:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "signal", signal);
340:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "network", network);
341: 
342:     // Chip temperature
343:     float temp = 0.0f;
344:     if (board.GetTemperature(temp))
345:     {
346:         auto chip = cJSON_CreateObject();
347:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(chip, "temperature", temp);
348:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "chip", chip);
349:     }
350: 
351:     auto str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
352:     std::string result(str);
353:     cJSON_free(str);
354:     cJSON_Delete(root);
355:     return result;
356: }

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code is semantically identical to the ground truth. Both versions:
- Retrieve the singleton instance of SsidManager
- Check if the SSID list is non-empty to determine `have_ssid`
- If SSIDs exist: log a message, start a timer with the same timeout constant, and initiate station mode via WifiManager
- If no SSIDs exist: delay for 1500ms using FreeRTOS vTaskDelay and then start WiFi configuration mode

Structurally, the two implementations are indistinguishable except for minor formatting differences (brace placement), which do not affect functionality or readability. All control flows, conditionals, API calls (ESP_LOGI, esp_timer_start_once, WifiManager::GetInstance(), etc.), and FreeRTOS/ESP-IDF specific functions are preserved exactly.

Functionally, all key operations from the ground truth are fully covered in the generated version without any omissions or deviations. Constants like CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC and pdMS_TO_TICKS are used identically, and the overall behavior of the function remains completely consistent.

It only implements a small part of the intended behavior — specifically, it calls an optional callback at the end, but completely omits all event-specific logic such as handling different NetworkEvent cases (e.g., Connected, Scanning, Disconnected), managing timers, updating internal state like in_config_mode_, or performing actions such as deinitializing Blufi or attempting reconnection. This results in fundamentally different behavior from the expected implementation.

Structurally, the function signature matches the ground truth exactly, which gives some credit for similarity. However, the body of the function lacks any control structures (like switch-case), logging calls, ESP-IDF API usage, or conditional blocks that are central to the reference solution. Therefore, while the outer form aligns, the inner structure diverges significantly.

In terms of functional completeness, the generated code misses almost all core functionality: no event handling, no side effects based on event type, no management of Wi-Fi states, and no integration with system components like timers or provisioning modules. Only the optional external notification callback is preserved, making the output highly incomplete relative to the full specification.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (100): The generated code has identical semantic meaning to the ground truth. Both functions cast the void pointer to WifiBoard*, log a warning message about WiFi connection timeout, stop the WiFi station through WifiManager, and start the WiFi configuration mode. The logical flow and intent are perfectly preserved.

Structure Similarity (95): The code structure is nearly identical. The function signature matches exactly, control flow is the same, and all API calls are preserved. The only differences are stylistic: the generated code uses additional spacing around pointer operators (void *arg vs void* arg, auto *board vs auto* board) and includes optional comment lines. These are minor formatting differences that don't affect functionality.

Functional Completeness (100): The generated code covers all key functionalities present in the ground truth. It performs the exact same operations: argument casting, logging, stopping the station, and starting configuration mode. No functionality is missing or altered.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code captures the basic idea of starting a WiFi configuration mode by notifying the user and calling a method on WifiManager. However, it lacks the detailed state management and multi-modal provisioning support found in the ground truth.

Semantic issues include:
- Missing state update (in_config_mode_ = true)
- Missing device state transition via Application::SetDeviceState()
- No handling of different provisioning methods (hotspot, Blufi, acoustic)
- No scheduled alert or user guidance for connecting to the hotspot
- No audio-based provisioning task creation

Structural differences:
- Function signature matches but internal logic diverges significantly
- Uses GetDisplay()->ShowNotification() instead of more complex alert mechanism
- Calls WifiManager::StartConfigurationMode() vs multiple specific methods
- Lacks conditional compilation directives (#ifdef blocks)
- No use of lambdas, tasks, or advanced scheduling

Functional incompleteness:
- Only implements one path of execution instead of three possible provisioning paths
- Omits critical integration points like Bluetooth initialization (Blufi) and acoustic provisioning
- Misses logging granularity and contextual information
- Lacks proper resource handling (task creation, channel info passing)
- No URL/SSID presentation to user for web-based configuration

Overall, while the core concept is preserved, many platform-specific features and robustness mechanisms are missing.

qualitative.analysis:
The generated code captures only the most basic functionality of entering Wi-Fi config mode but misses several critical aspects present in the ground truth:

Semantic Correctness (40/100):
- The generated code has the correct high-level goal but lacks proper state management and error handling.
- It omits the important conditional logic based on device state which determines whether to perform immediate config mode entry or delay it.
- Missing graceful handling of ongoing operations like speaking/listening that need to be stopped before entering config mode.
- No task creation for delayed execution when device is in active states.

Structure Similarity (50/100):
- Function signature matches correctly.
- Some API calls are similar (StopStation, StartWifiConfigMode).
- However, missing key structural elements like conditional blocks, logging, task creation, timer stopping, and integration with Application/Display systems.
- Control flow is significantly simplified compared to ground truth.

Functional Completeness (30/100):
- Missing core functionalities such as:
  * Device state checking and conditional behavior
  * Protocol reset via Application::ResetProtocol()
  * Display notification through GetDisplay()->ShowNotification()
  * Delayed execution using xTaskCreate for graceful shutdown
  * Proper timer management with esp_timer_stop()
  * Logging functionality
- The generated code represents less than 30% of the actual required functionality.

330: std::string WifiBoard::GetDeviceStatusJson()
331: {
332:     auto &board = Board::GetInstance();
333:     auto root = cJSON_CreateObject();
334: 
335:     // Audio speaker
336:     auto audio_speaker = cJSON_CreateObject();
337:     if (auto codec = board.GetAudioCodec())
338:     {

340:     }
341:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_speaker", audio_speaker);
342: 
343:     // Screen
344:     auto screen = cJSON_CreateObject();
345:     if (auto backlight = board.GetBacklight())
346:     {
347:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(screen, "brightness", backlight->brightness());
348:     }
349:     if (auto display = board.GetDisplay(); display && display->height() > 64)
350:     {
351:         if (auto theme = display->GetTheme())
352:         {
353:             cJSON_AddStringToObject(screen, "theme", theme->name().c_str());
354:         }
355:     }
356:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "screen", screen);
357: 
358:     // Battery
359:     int level = 0;
360:     bool charging = false, discharging = false;
361:     if (board.GetBatteryLevel(level, charging, discharging))
362:     {
363:         auto battery = cJSON_CreateObject();
364:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(battery, "level", level);
365:         cJSON_AddBoolToObject(battery, "charging", charging);
366:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "battery", battery);
367:     }
368: 
369:     // Network
370:     auto &wifi = WifiManager::GetInstance();
371:     auto network = cJSON_CreateObject();
372:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "type", "wifi");
373:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "ssid", wifi.GetSsid().c_str());
374:     int rssi = wifi.GetRssi();
375:     const char *signal = rssi >= -60 ? "strong" : (rssi >= -70 ? "medium" : "weak");
376:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(network, "signal", signal);
377:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "network", network);
378: 
379:     // Chip temperature
380:     float temp = 0.0f;
381:     if (board.GetTemperature(temp))
382:     {
383:         auto chip = cJSON_CreateObject();
384:         cJSON_AddNumberToObject(chip, "temperature", temp);
385:         cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "chip", chip);
386:     }
387: 
388:     auto str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
389:     std::string result(str);
390:     cJSON_free(str);
391:     cJSON_Delete(root);
392:     return result;
393: }
394: 
</file>

Both aim to provide a single network interface instance, but the implementation approaches differ significantly. The generated code assumes WifiManager class exists and has GetInstance() method, which may not be equivalent to the EspNetwork instantiation in the ground truth.

Structure Similarity (70): Both functions have the same signature (NetworkInterface* WifiBoard::GetNetwork()), same return type, and both return a pointer to a network interface object. However, the implementation differs - ground truth uses static variable declaration and direct instantiation, while generated code uses a singleton pattern call. The basic function structure and return mechanism are preserved.

Functional Completeness (40): The generated code misses the specific EspNetwork instantiation that's central to the ground truth implementation. It relies on an external WifiManager class that may not exist or may not provide the same functionality. The generated code doesn't demonstrate the same initialization approach or guarantee the same type of network interface object, potentially missing platform-specific functionality that EspNetwork provides.

The generated code is identical to the ground truth in terms of semantic meaning and logical flow. Both implementations follow the exact same sequence of operations:

3. Check if not connected (return FONT_AWESOME_WIFI_SLASH)
4. Get RSSI value and categorize into three strength levels

The only differences are purely stylistic - the generated code uses different brace placement (Allman style vs K&R style) and spacing around the reference operator (&). These formatting differences do not affect the code's functionality or structure.

All key functionalities are present including:
- Proper function signature matching
- Same conditional logic and order
- Identical API calls (IsConfigMode, IsConnected, GetRssi)
- Same return values for each condition
- Equivalent control flow structure

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code does not preserve the exact semantics of the ground truth. While both functions aim to produce a JSON representation of board information, the structure and content differ significantly.
- The ground truth returns a flat JSON object containing type, name, ssid, rssi, channel, ip, and mac fields directly under the root.
- In contrast, the generated code creates a nested JSON structure with "network", "capabilities", and "power" sub-objects, which changes the meaning and usage of the output data.
- Additionally, the board type in the generated code is hardcoded as "wifi", whereas the ground truth uses a compile-time constant BOARD_TYPE.

Structure Similarity:
- The function signature differs: the generated code omits the redundant parentheses in the declaration (`GetBoardJson()()` vs `GetBoardJson()`).
- Control flow varies greatly; the ground truth uses simple string concatenation with conditional logic for including Wi-Fi details only when not in config mode.
- The generated code uses cJSON library APIs extensively, creating objects and arrays dynamically, which is structurally very different from direct string construction.
- Conditional compilation sections are introduced in the generated version that do not exist in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code includes additional functionality such as capabilities listing and power save mode reporting, which go beyond what's required by the task or present in the ground truth.
- However, critical elements like BOARD_NAME and conditional inclusion of Wi-Fi parameters based on configuration mode (IsConfigMode) are missing or implemented differently.
- Some core Wi-Fi properties like ssid, rssi, channel, ip, and mac are included but placed inside a nested "network" object instead of at the top level.
- Use of external libraries (cJSON) adds complexity not implied by the original implementation.

Semantic Correctness (30/100): The generated code attempts to implement WiFi power saving functionality but uses a different approach than the ground truth. While both functions aim to set a power save level, the generated code directly calls ESP-IDF APIs (esp_wifi_set_ps), whereas the ground truth maps an external enum to an internal one and delegates to a WifiManager singleton. The semantic mapping between PowerSaveLevel values differs significantly — the ground truth has LOW_POWER, BALANCED, PERFORMANCE, while the generated code uses kNone, kLight, kMedium, kHigh. Structure Similarity (40/100): Both codes feature the same function signature and use a switch-case control flow to handle different power save levels. However, the generated code includes ESP-IDF specific logging and API calls that are not present in the ground truth. The ground truth follows a pattern of enum mapping followed by delegation to a manager class, which is completely absent in the generated code. The handling of default cases also differs in approach.

Functional Completeness (25/100): The generated code implements direct WiFi power management using ESP-IDF APIs, which might be functionally valid for an ESP32 system but completely misses the abstraction layer and delegation pattern used in the ground truth. It lacks the enum mapping logic and does not call WifiManager::GetInstance().SetPowerSaveLevel() at all. Additionally, the generated code's implementation doesn't align with the expected interface since it assumes different enum values (kNone, kLight, etc.) rather than the ones shown in the ground truth (LOW_POWER, BALANCED, PERFORMANCE). Critical functional elements like the WifiManager interaction are missing entirely.

The generated implementation would not fulfill the actual requirements of a comprehensive device status reporting system.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness (75): The generated code captures the core blinking logic but has some semantic differences. The ground truth uses bitwise AND (`blink_counter_ & 1`) for odd/even checking while generated code uses modulo. More significantly, the generated code has opposite logic - it turns LED ON when counter is even, while ground truth does so when odd. The generated code also introduces a null check and BLINK_INFINITE concept not present in ground truth.

Structure Similarity (60): Function signature matches but internal structure differs significantly. Ground truth uses if-else for LED operations followed by conditional refresh, while generated code always calls refresh. Control flow order is different - ground truth decrements first, then checks conditions. Generated code has additional null check and BLINK_INFINITE handling that changes the structure.

Functional Completeness (85): Generated code covers most key functionalities including mutex locking, LED strip operations, counter decrementing, and timer stopping. However, it's missing the exact timing of when refresh is called (ground truth only refreshes when setting pixel, not when clearing) and doesn't replicate the precise counter management logic from ground truth.

The generated code captures much of the intended logic for handling different device states, such as turning the LED off/on, setting colors, and starting blinking patterns based on the device state. However, it omits several important cases present in the ground truth, including `kDeviceStateStarting` and `kDeviceStateWifiConfiguring`. These missing states result in incomplete behavior when compared to the reference implementation.

Additionally, there are discrepancies in how the application instance is accessed. In the ground truth, `Application::GetInstance()` is used to retrieve the current device state and other properties like `IsVoiceDetected()`, but this pattern is not followed correctly in the generated version — particularly because `app` is referenced without being declared or obtained from a singleton access point.

From a structural perspective, the function signature differs significantly; the ground truth uses `void SingleLed::OnStateChanged()` with no parameters, relying internally on fetching the device state via the Application singleton, whereas the generated code expects a `DeviceState` parameter directly. This mismatch affects both usage context and encapsulation design.

Control flow constructs like switch-case statements are preserved reasonably well, though some log messages differ slightly in wording (`"Unknown device state"` vs `"Unknown led strip event"`), which虽 minor, contributes to divergence.

Lastly, critical functionality around blink timing during startup/config stages is completely absent from the generated code, reducing its functional completeness relative to the original.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The core functionality of initializing an event group and setting up a reconnect timer is preserved.
- Both versions use a lambda callback to handle reconnection logic.
- However, the generated code omits checking the device state (`kDeviceStateIdle`) before attempting reconnection, which may lead to different behavior under certain conditions.
- The generated version directly calls `StartMqttClient`, while the ground truth schedules it via `Application::Schedule`. This difference could affect thread safety or execution context handling.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature and overall structure are nearly identical.
- Use of `esp_timer_create_args_t` and `esp_timer_create()` is consistent.
- Callback definition style using lambdas is maintained.
- Minor differences include initialization order and additional fields like `.name` in the generated code.

Functional Completeness:
- The generated code includes extra initializations (e.g., `mqtt_.reset()`, `udp_.reset()`, audio-related variables), suggesting broader scope but not required by the task.
- It lacks critical logic such as scheduling through `Application::Schedule` and device state checking.
- Shared pointer management for `alive_` is handled differently but arguably more safely in the generated version.
- Missing integration with application-level coordination present in the ground truth affects completeness regarding intended system behavior.

53: bool MqttProtocol::StartMqttClient(bool report_error)
54: {
55:     if (mqtt_ != nullptr)
56:     {
57:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Mqtt client already started");
58:         mqtt_.reset();
59:     }
60: 
61:     Settings settings("mqtt", false);
62:     auto endpoint = settings.GetString("endpoint");
63:     auto client_id = settings.GetString("client_id");
64:     auto username = settings.GetString("username");
65:     auto password = settings.GetString("password");
66:     int keepalive_interval = settings.GetInt("keepalive", 240);
67:     publish_topic_ = settings.GetString("publish_topic");
68: 
69:     if (endpoint.empty())
70:     {
71:         ESP_LOGW(TAG, "MQTT endpoint is not specified");
72:         if (report_error)
73:         {
74:             SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_FOUND);
75:         }
76:         return false;
77:     }
78: 
79:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
80:     mqtt_ = network->CreateMqtt(0);
81:     mqtt_->SetKeepAlive(keepalive_interval);
82: 
83:     mqtt_->OnDisconnected([this]()
84:                           {
85:         if (on_disconnected_ != nullptr) {
86:             on_disconnected_();
87:         }
88:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT disconnected, schedule reconnect in %d seconds", MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS / 1000);
89:         esp_timer_start_once(reconnect_timer_, MQTT_RECONNECT_INTERVAL_MS * 1000); });
90: 
91:     mqtt_->OnConnected([this]()
92:                        {
93:         if (on_connected_ != nullptr) {
94:             on_connected_();
95:         }
96:         esp_timer_stop(reconnect_timer_); });
97: 
98:     mqtt_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &topic, const std::string &payload)
99:                      {
100:         cJSON* root = cJSON_Parse(payload.c_str());
101:         if (root == nullptr) {
102:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to parse json message %s", payload.c_str());
103:             return;
104:         }
105:         cJSON* type = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "type");
106:         if (!cJSON_IsString(type)) {
107:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Message type is invalid");
108:             cJSON_Delete(root);
109:             return;
110:         }
111: 
112:         if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "hello") == 0) {
113:             ParseServerHello(root);
114:         } else if (strcmp(type->valuestring, "goodbye") == 0) {
115:             auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
116:             ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Received goodbye message, session_id: %s", session_id ? session_id->valuestring : "null");
117:             if (session_id == nullptr || session_id_ == session_id->valuestring) {
118:                 auto alive = alive_;  // Capture alive flag
119:                 Application::GetInstance().Schedule([this, alive]() {
120:                     if (*alive) {
121:                         CloseAudioChannel();
122:                     }
123:                 });
124:             }
125:         } else if (on_incoming_json_ != nullptr) {
126:             on_incoming_json_(root);
127:         }
128:         cJSON_Delete(root);
129:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
130: 
131:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connecting to endpoint %s", endpoint.c_str());
132:     std::string broker_address;
133:     int broker_port = 8883;
134:     size_t pos = endpoint.find(':');
135:     if (pos != std::string::npos)
136:     {
137:         broker_address = endpoint.substr(0, pos);
138:         broker_port = std::stoi(endpoint.substr(pos + 1));
139:     }
140:     else
141:     {
142:         broker_address = endpoint;
143:     }
144:     if (!mqtt_->Connect(broker_address, broker_port, client_id, username, password))
145:     {
146:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to connect to endpoint, code=%d", mqtt_->GetLastError());
147:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_CONNECTED);
148:         return false;
149:     }
150: 
151:     ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Connected to endpoint");
152:     return true;

155: bool MqttProtocol::SendText(const std::string &text)
156: {

170: bool MqttProtocol::SendAudio(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
171: {
172:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
173:     if (udp_ == nullptr)
174:     {
175:         return false;
176:     }
177: 
178:     std::string nonce(aes_nonce_);
179:     *(uint16_t *)&nonce[2] = htons(packet->payload.size());
180:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[8] = htonl(packet->timestamp);
181:     *(uint32_t *)&nonce[12] = htonl(++local_sequence_);
182: 
183:     std::string encrypted;
184:     encrypted.resize(aes_nonce_.size() + packet->payload.size());
185:     memcpy(encrypted.data(), nonce.data(), nonce.size());
186: 
187:     size_t nc_off = 0;
188:     uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
189:     if (mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, packet->payload.size(), &nc_off, (uint8_t *)nonce.c_str(), stream_block,
190:                               (uint8_t *)packet->payload.data(), (uint8_t *)&encrypted[nonce.size()]) != 0)
191:     {
192:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encrypt audio data");
193:         return false;
194:     }
195: 
196:     return udp_->Send(encrypted) > 0;
197: }

199: void MqttProtocol::CloseAudioChannel()
200: {
201:     {
202:         std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
203:         udp_.reset();
204:     }
205: 
206:     std::string message = "{";
207:     message += "\"session_id\":\"" + session_id_ + "\",";
208:     message += "\"type\":\"goodbye\"";
209:     message += "}";
210:     SendText(message);
211: 
212:     if (on_audio_channel_closed_ != nullptr)
213:     {
214:         on_audio_channel_closed_();
215:     }
216: }

218: bool MqttProtocol::OpenAudioChannel()
219: {
220:     if (mqtt_ == nullptr || !mqtt_->IsConnected())
221:     {
222:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "MQTT is not connected, try to connect now");
223:         if (!StartMqttClient(true))
224:         {
225:             return false;
226:         }
227:     }
228: 
229:     error_occurred_ = false;
230:     session_id_ = "";
231:     xEventGroupClearBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
232: 
233:     auto message = GetHelloMessage();
234:     if (!SendText(message))
235:     {
236:         return false;
237:     }
238: 
239:     // 等待服务器响应
240:     EventBits_t bits = xEventGroupWaitBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT, pdTRUE, pdFALSE, pdMS_TO_TICKS(10000));
241:     if (!(bits & MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT))
242:     {
243:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to receive server hello");
244:         SetError(Lang::Strings::SERVER_TIMEOUT);
245:         return false;
246:     }
247: 
248:     std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(channel_mutex_);
249:     auto network = Board::GetInstance().GetNetwork();
250:     udp_ = network->CreateUdp(2);
251:     udp_->OnMessage([this](const std::string &data)
252:                     {
253:         /*
254:          * UDP Encrypted OPUS Packet Format:
255:          * |type 1u|flags 1u|payload_len 2u|ssrc 4u|timestamp 4u|sequence 4u|
256:          * |payload payload_len|
257:          */
258:         if (data.size() < sizeof(aes_nonce_)) {
259:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet size: %u", data.size());
260:             return;
261:         }
262:         if (data[0] != 0x01) {
263:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Invalid audio packet type: %x", data[0]);
264:             return;
265:         }
266:         uint32_t timestamp = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[8]);
267:         uint32_t sequence = ntohl(*(uint32_t*)&data[12]);
268:         if (sequence < remote_sequence_) {
269:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with old sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_);
270:             return;
271:         }
272:         if (sequence != remote_sequence_ + 1) {
273:             ESP_LOGW(TAG, "Received audio packet with wrong sequence: %lu, expected: %lu", sequence, remote_sequence_ + 1);
274:         }
275: 
276:         size_t decrypted_size = data.size() - aes_nonce_.size();
277:         size_t nc_off = 0;
278:         uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
279:         auto nonce = (uint8_t*)data.data();
280:         auto encrypted = (uint8_t*)data.data() + aes_nonce_.size();
281:         auto packet = std::make_unique<AudioStreamPacket>();
282:         packet->sample_rate = server_sample_rate_;
283:         packet->frame_duration = server_frame_duration_;
284:         packet->timestamp = timestamp;
285:         packet->payload.resize(decrypted_size);
286:         int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, decrypted_size, &nc_off, nonce, stream_block, encrypted, (uint8_t*)packet->payload.data());
287:         if (ret != 0) {
288:             ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to decrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
289:             return;
290:         }
291:         if (on_incoming_audio_ != nullptr) {
292:             on_incoming_audio_(std::move(packet));
293:         }
294:         remote_sequence_ = sequence;
295:         last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now(); });
296: 
297:     udp_->Connect(udp_server_, udp_port_);
298: 
299:     if (on_audio_channel_opened_ != nullptr)
300:     {
301:         on_audio_channel_opened_();
302:     }
303:     return true;
304: }
305: 
306: std::string MqttProtocol::GetHelloMessage()
307: {
308:     // 发送 hello 消息申请 UDP 通道
309:     cJSON *root = cJSON_CreateObject();
310:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "type", "hello");
311:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(root, "version", 3);
312:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(root, "transport", "udp");
313:     cJSON *features = cJSON_CreateObject();
314: #if CONFIG_USE_SERVER_AEC
315:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "aec", true);
316: #endif
317:     cJSON_AddBoolToObject(features, "mcp", true);
318:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "features", features);
319:     cJSON *audio_params = cJSON_CreateObject();
320:     cJSON_AddStringToObject(audio_params, "format", "opus");
321:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "sample_rate", 16000);
322:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "channels", 1);
323:     cJSON_AddNumberToObject(audio_params, "frame_duration", OPUS_FRAME_DURATION_MS);
324:     cJSON_AddItemToObject(root, "audio_params", audio_params);
325:     auto json_str = cJSON_PrintUnformatted(root);
326:     std::string message(json_str);
327:     cJSON_free(json_str);
328:     cJSON_Delete(root);
329:     return message;
330: }
331: 
332: void MqttProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
333: {
334:     auto transport = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "transport");
335:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "udp") != 0)
336:     {
337:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Unsupported transport: %s", transport->valuestring);
338:         return;
339:     }
340: 
341:     auto session_id = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "session_id");
342:     if (cJSON_IsString(session_id))
343:     {
344:         session_id_ = session_id->valuestring;
345:         ESP_LOGI(TAG, "Session ID: %s", session_id_.c_str());
346:     }
347: 
348:     // Get sample rate from hello message
349:     auto audio_params = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "audio_params");
350:     if (cJSON_IsObject(audio_params))
351:     {
352:         auto sample_rate = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "sample_rate");
353:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(sample_rate))
354:         {
355:             server_sample_rate_ = sample_rate->valueint;
356:         }
357:         auto frame_duration = cJSON_GetObjectItem(audio_params, "frame_duration");
358:         if (cJSON_IsNumber(frame_duration))
359:         {
360:             server_frame_duration_ = frame_duration->valueint;
361:         }
362:     }
363: 
364:     auto udp = cJSON_GetObjectItem(root, "udp");
365:     if (!cJSON_IsObject(udp))
366:     {
367:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "UDP is not specified");
368:         return;
369:     }
370:     udp_server_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "server")->valuestring;
371:     udp_port_ = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "port")->valueint;
372:     auto key = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "key")->valuestring;
373:     auto nonce = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "nonce")->valuestring;
374: 
375:     // auto encryption = cJSON_GetObjectItem(udp, "encryption")->valuestring;
376:     // ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UDP server: %s, port: %d, encryption: %s", udp_server_.c_str(), udp_port_, encryption);
377:     aes_nonce_ = DecodeHexString(nonce);
378:     mbedtls_aes_init(&aes_ctx_);
379:     mbedtls_aes_setkey_enc(&aes_ctx_, (const unsigned char *)DecodeHexString(key).c_str(), 128);
380:     local_sequence_ = 0;
381:     remote_sequence_ = 0;
382:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
383: }
384: 
385: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
386: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
387: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
388: {
389:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
390:         return c - '0';
391:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
392:         return c - 'A' + 10;
393:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
394:         return c - 'a' + 10;
395:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
396: }
397: 
398: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
399: {
400:     std::string decoded;
401:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
402:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
403:     {
404:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
405:         decoded.push_back(byte);
406:     }
407:     return decoded;
408: }
409: 
410: bool MqttProtocol::IsAudioChannelOpened() const
411: {
412:     return udp_ != nullptr && !error_occurred_ && !IsTimeout();
413: }
414: 
</file>

Semantic Correctness (85): The generated code correctly implements the destructor's main purpose - cleaning up resources and marking the object as dead. The logical flow is mostly correct, with proper null checks before operations. However, there are some issues: 1) The alive_ check is more defensive in the generated code but functionally equivalent, 2) The order of operations differs slightly (UDP cleanup before MQTT in ground truth vs MQTT before UDP in generated), 3) The generated code includes an additional mbedtls_aes_free call not present in ground truth which could be problematic if not needed.

Structure Similarity (80): Both codes follow similar patterns with null checks and resource cleanup. The function signature is correct, and both use the same API calls (esp_timer_stop, esp_timer_delete, vEventGroupDelete, reset()). However, there are structural differences: 1) Different ordering of cleanup operations, 2) Additional null assignment (reconnect_timer_ = nullptr, event_group_handle_ = nullptr) in generated code, 3) Extra cleanup for aes_ctx_ in generated code, 4) Slightly different comment styles and formatting.

Functional Completeness (75): The generated code covers most key functionalities but has notable differences: 1) Missing ESP_LOGI debug message, 2) Includes extra mbedtls_aes_free operation not in ground truth, 3) Different order of udp_ and mqtt_ cleanup, 4) Additional null assignments that aren't harmful but not present in ground truth. The core functionality (marking as dead, timer cleanup, resource deletion) is present but with some deviations.

247: void MqttProtocol::ParseServerHello(const cJSON *root)
248: {

250:     if (transport == nullptr || strcmp(transport->valuestring, "udp") != 0)

253:         return;

283:         return;

291:     // ESP_LOGI(TAG, "UDP server: %s, port: %d, encryption: %s", udp_server_.c_str(), udp_port_, encryption);
292:     aes_nonce_ = DecodeHexString(nonce);
293:     mbedtls_aes_init(&aes_ctx_);
294:     mbedtls_aes_setkey_enc(&aes_ctx_, (const unsigned char *)DecodeHexString(key).c_str(), 128);
295:     local_sequence_ = 0;
296:     remote_sequence_ = 0;
297:     xEventGroupSetBits(event_group_handle_, MQTT_PROTOCOL_SERVER_HELLO_EVENT);
298: }

300: static const char hex_chars[] = "0123456789ABCDEF";
301: // 辅助函数，将单个十六进制字符转换为对应的数值
302: static inline uint8_t CharToHex(char c)
303: {
304:     if (c >= '0' && c <= '9')
305:         return c - '0';
306:     if (c >= 'A' && c <= 'F')
307:         return c - 'A' + 10;
308:     if (c >= 'a' && c <= 'f')
309:         return c - 'a' + 10;
310:     return 0; // 对于无效输入，返回0
311: }
312: 
313: std::string MqttProtocol::DecodeHexString(const std::string &hex_string)
314: {
315:     std::string decoded;
316:     decoded.reserve(hex_string.size() / 2);
317:     for (size_t i = 0; i < hex_string.size(); i += 2)
318:     {
319:         char byte = (CharToHex(hex_string[i]) << 4) | CharToHex(hex_string[i + 1]);
320:         decoded.push_back(byte);
321:     }
322:     return decoded;
323: }
324: 
325: bool MqttProtocol::IsAudioChannelOpened() const
326: {
327:     return udp_ != nullptr && !error_occurred_ && !IsTimeout();
328: }

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code attempts to start an MQTT client based on configuration settings, which aligns with the intent of the ground truth.
- However, there are significant differences in how configurations are retrieved and processed. The ground truth uses a `Settings` class directly querying specific keys like "endpoint", while the generated code assumes structured access via `settings->network.mqtt.*`.
- Error handling differs; the ground truth logs warnings or errors conditionally using `report_error`, whereas the generated code unconditionally sets generic server errors.
- The logic for parsing broker address and port is missing in the generated version, which affects correctness when endpoints do not include ports.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches (`bool StartMqttClient(bool)`), but internal implementation diverges significantly.
- Control flow varies: ground truth checks for existing connections differently, does not use event groups or timers for synchronization, and parses JSON messages inline rather than delegating to another method.
- API usage differs notably — e.g., `esp_timer_start_once` vs custom timer management, `xEventGroupWaitBits` vs direct callback-based connection confirmation.
- Generated code introduces concepts like `HandleMqttMessage()` that aren't present in the ground truth, altering structural fidelity.

Functional Completeness:
- Both versions initialize/configure and connect an MQTT client, handle connect/disconnect callbacks, and manage subscriptions.
- Missing features in generated code include proper error string localization (uses generic strings instead of `Lang::Strings::SERVER_NOT_FOUND` etc.), detailed logging around connection states, and parsing of incoming JSON payloads for specific message types such as 'hello' or 'goodbye'.
- The generated code adds some functionality absent from the ground truth, such as setting last will testament and waiting for connection events via FreeRTOS event groups, indicating partial over-completion in non-core areas.

qualitative.analysis:
Semantic Correctness:
- The generated code correctly implements the core logic of sending an audio packet over UDP with encryption.
- Both versions use AES-CTR encryption and handle mutex locking for thread safety.
- However, there are differences in how headers are constructed. Ground truth uses a fixed 'nonce' structure with specific fields overwritten, while generated code builds a more detailed header including type, flags, ssrc, etc.
- The generated code includes additional validations (null packet, empty payload) and logging which aren't present in the ground truth but don't contradict it.

Structure Similarity:
- Function signature matches exactly.
- Control flow is similar: validation → lock → prepare data → encrypt → send.
- Key API calls like mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr and udp_->Send are used in both.
- Major structural difference lies in header construction: ground truth modifies parts of a predefined nonce, whereas generated constructs a full protocol header manually.
- Use of std::vector vs std::string for buffers differs slightly in approach but achieves the same purpose.
- Generated code has extra features such as updating last_incoming_time_, which isn't in the ground truth.

Functional Completeness:
- All essential operations from the ground truth are implemented: mutex protection, encryption using AES-CTR, sequence number incrementing, timestamp handling, and UDP transmission.
- Additional functionality in generated code (e.g., enhanced error checking, richer packet header) exceeds the minimal requirements implied by the ground truth.
- One minor discrepancy: the Send() call in ground truth returns an integer compared against > 0, while generated assumes a boolean return; this could be a compatibility issue depending on actual UDP interface.

183: bool MqttProtocol::SendAudio(std::unique_ptr<AudioStreamPacket> packet)
184: {
185:     if (!IsAudioChannelOpened())
186:     {
187:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Audio channel is not opened");
188:         return false;
189:     }
190: 
191:     if (packet == nullptr || packet->payload.empty())
192:         uint16_t payload_len = packet->payload.size();
193:     uint32_t ssrc = htonl(0x12345678); // Fixed SSRC
194:     uint32_t timestamp = htonl(packet->timestamp);
195:     uint32_t sequence = htonl(++local_sequence_);
196: 
197:     // Create plaintext packet with header
198:     std::vector<uint8_t> plaintext(sizeof(uint8_t) + sizeof(uint8_t) + sizeof(uint16_t) +
199:                                    sizeof(uint32_t) + sizeof(uint32_t) + sizeof(uint32_t) + payload_len);
200: 
201:     size_t offset = 0;
202: 
203:     // Type (0x01 for audio data)
204:     plaintext[offset++] = 0x01;
205: 
206:     // Flags (0x00)
207:     plaintext[offset++] = 0x00;
208: 
209:     // Payload length (network byte order)
210:     uint16_t net_payload_len = htons(payload_len);
211:     memcpy(plaintext.data() + offset, &net_payload_len, sizeof(uint16_t));
212:     offset += sizeof(uint16_t);
213: 
214:     // SSRC
215:     memcpy(plaintext.data() + offset, &ssrc, sizeof(uint32_t));
216:     offset += sizeof(uint32_t);
217: 
218:     // Timestamp
219:     memcpy(plaintext.data() + offset, &timestamp, sizeof(uint32_t));
220:     offset += sizeof(uint32_t);
221: 
222:     // Sequence number
223:     memcpy(plaintext.data() + offset, &sequence, sizeof(uint32_t));
224:     offset += sizeof(uint32_t);
225: 
226:     // Payload
227:     memcpy(plaintext.data() + offset, packet->payload.data(), payload_len);
228: 
229:     // Encrypt the packet
230:     size_t encrypted_size = plaintext.size();
231:     std::vector<uint8_t> encrypted_packet(aes_nonce_.size() + encrypted_size);
232: 
233:     // Copy nonce to the beginning of the packet
234:     memcpy(encrypted_packet.data(), aes_nonce_.data(), aes_nonce_.size());
235: 
236:     // Encrypt the plaintext
237:     size_t nc_off = 0;
238:     uint8_t stream_block[16] = {0};
239:     int ret = mbedtls_aes_crypt_ctr(&aes_ctx_, encrypted_size, &nc_off,
240:                                     (uint8_t *)aes_nonce_.data(), stream_block,
241:                                     plaintext.data(),
242:                                     encrypted_packet.data() + aes_nonce_.size());
243: 
244:     if (ret != 0)
245:     {
246:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to encrypt audio data, ret: %d", ret);
247:         return false;
248:     }
249: 
250:     // Send the encrypted packet
251:     if (!udp_->Send((char *)encrypted_packet.data(), encrypted_packet.size()))
252:     {
253:         ESP_LOGE(TAG, "Failed to send audio packet");
254:         return false;
255:     }
256: 
257:     last_incoming_time_ = std::chrono::steady_clock::now();
258:     return true;
259: }
